雲昌寺.tagline: Discover a breathtaking sea of deep blue hydrangeas at this ancient temple by the Oga Sea.
雲昌寺.fee: 大人 500円～1,000円（視開花狀況調整）
雲昌寺.address: 秋田県男鹿市北浦北浦字北浦57
雲昌寺.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from JR Oga Line "Oga Station"
雲昌寺.access_car: About 40 minutes from Showa Oga Peninsula IC on the Akita Expressway
雲昌寺.desc:
Located on the Oga Peninsula in Akita Prefecture, Unshoji (うんしょうじ) has been awarded the top honor as Japan's "Must-See Scenic Spot Before You Die." When the flowers bloom in late June to mid-July, the entire temple grounds transform into a stunning, vibrant blue, resembling a thick, velvety carpet of color, earning it the nickname "Paradise of Blue."

As you stroll through this azure wonderland, take a moment to appreciate the grand views, but also keep an eye out for delightful hidden surprises. Look down at the stone path to discover a romantic "heart-shaped stone" tucked away, and throughout the garden, you'll find six charming Jizo statues, each with unique expressions, perfect for a leisurely exploration.

During the blooming season (typically from mid-June to mid-July), the temple offers separate tickets for "daytime viewing" and "night illumination," which cannot be used interchangeably. Ticket prices vary based on the blooming status (approximately 500 to 1,200 yen). If you're driving, be aware that during peak bloom, general vehicles are prohibited from entering the road in front of the temple, so you’ll need to park at nearby paid lots and walk to the site.

陸奧紫陽花園.tagline: Discover Japan's only hydrangea path beneath towering cedar trees, showcasing a magical floating flower pond in late bloom.
陸奧紫陽花園.types: 400 varieties including Yamazaki hydrangea, Western hydrangea, and Annabelle
陸奧紫陽花園.address: 岩手県一関市舞川字原沢111
陸奧紫陽花園.access_train: About 20 minutes by taxi from JR Ichinoseki Station
陸奧紫陽花園.access_car: About 30 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway Ichinoseki IC
陸奧紫陽花園.desc:
Recognized by the Japan Hydrangea Association as the "best in Japan," the Mutsu Hydrangea Garden is a hidden gem that every traveler must visit. Its unique charm lies in being the only place in Japan where majestic cedar forests and vibrant hydrangeas are perfectly intertwined. When sunlight filters through the towering cedars and dances upon the sprawling purple and blue petals, it feels like stepping into a fairytale.

As the garden features mostly primitive mountain trails, families with elders or children are strongly encouraged to reserve the limited "mini garden cars" in advance, which provide a comfortable guided tour around the area. The most breathtaking highlight of the blooming season is undoubtedly the limited-time appearance of the "hydrangea pond" in late bloom! The garden staff decorates the pond with countless colorful fallen petals, creating a vibrant floating flower carpet that is an absolute must-see for any traveler.

The Mutsu Hydrangea Garden is open only from late June to late July each year. Please note that the garden operates on a "dynamic pricing system," with ticket prices adjusting based on bloom conditions and the availability of the "hydrangea pond." Be sure to check the real-time bloom status for the day's ticket prices when planning your visit.

明月院.address: 神奈川県鎌倉市山ノ内189
明月院.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Yokosuka Line "Kitakamakura Station"
明月院.access_car: Public transportation is recommended (area often has restrictions)
明月院.desc:
Meigetsuin is an ancient temple in Kamakura belonging to the Rinzai sect. After the war, due to a lack of wooden stakes along the path, hydrangeas were planted as a border, unexpectedly becoming a symbol of the temple and earning the nickname "Hydrangea Temple." Within the temple grounds, approximately 2,500 plants bloom, with 95% being the "Hime Ajisai" variety. During the rainy season in June, the garden is adorned with a clear, deep blue hue, celebrated by many as the classic "Meigetsuin Blue."

The most famous scene is the stone path leading to the mountain gate, flanked by a sea of blue flowers that creates a dreamlike tunnel. The circular "Window of Enlightenment" in the main hall offers a stunning view of the serene backyard garden. As this location is one of Japan's most popular hydrangea spots, it can get quite crowded during the blooming season. To avoid the crowds and capture photos without anyone in the frame, it is highly recommended to arrive 30 minutes to an hour before opening time.

Access is very convenient; a 10-minute walk from JR Yokosuka Line's "Kitakamakura Station" will get you there. The regular admission fee is 500 yen, and the opening hours are from 9 AM to 4 PM. It’s important to note that during the peak hydrangea season in June, the temple often adjusts its opening hours flexibly, opening earlier at 8:30 AM and extending to 5 PM, so it's best to check in advance before your visit.
明月院.tagline: Stroll the iconic stone path of Meigetsuin, discovering the serene beauty of 2,500 hydrangeas in a seasonal masterpiece.

下田公園.types: Over 100 varieties including Western hydrangeas and amethyst hydrangeas
下田公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from Izukyu-Shimoda Station on the Izukyu Line
下田公園.access_car: Approximately a 1 hour and 40-minute drive from Numazu IC on the Tomei Expressway
下田公園.desc:
Shimoda Park, located on the Izu Peninsula in Shizuoka, was once the site of Shimoda Castle during the Warring States period. The park boasts an impressive 150,000 hydrangeas, totaling around 3 million blooms, making it one of the largest displays in Japan. Every June, the coastal hills are blanketed with a vibrant array of flowers, creating a spectacular scene surrounded by colorful blossoms.

The park's highlight is the breathtaking "interplay of sea views and flower fields." As you ascend the trails to the observation deck, you can take in the stunning sight of the multicolored hydrangeas, the azure waters of Shimoda Port, and the Izu Islands all at once. Walking through pathways lined with flowers taller than you is an unforgettable experience, enveloped by a wall of vibrant blooms.

Each June, the "Shimoda Hydrangea Festival" is held, featuring outdoor stalls selling local delicacies and souvenirs. Since the park is situated on a hill with many steps and slopes, wearing comfortable sneakers is highly recommended for flower viewing. If you want to avoid the crowds, early morning and evening are the best times for a leisurely stroll and photography.
下田公園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking sea of 150,000 hydrangeas, overlooking the stunning views of Shimoda Port and vibrant hills.

三室戶寺.types: Western hydrangeas, lacecap hydrangeas, oakleaf hydrangeas, and more
三室戶寺.address: 京都府宇治市菟道滋賀谷21
三室戶寺.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from Keihan "Sanzen-in Station"
三室戶寺.access_car: About 3 minutes from Keiji Bypass "Uji Higashi IC"
三室戶寺.desc:
Sanzen-in Temple, located in Uji, Kyoto, is renowned as the "Hydrangea Temple" of Kansai. The garden boasts around 20,000 hydrangeas across 50 different varieties, transforming into a colorful sea of blooms during the rainy season in late June to mid-July. One of the unique joys here is the quest for the naturally formed "heart-shaped hydrangeas," believed to bring good luck in love.

Highlights include the hydrangea garden adorned with a variety of flowers and stunning views that frame the classical garden and the three-story pagoda. During the later part of the blooming season, a special "Weekend Night Illumination" event takes place, where lights cast a magical and mysterious atmosphere over the nighttime flower sea, offering a completely different experience from the daytime.

As a top destination in Kansai, weekends during the blooming season can get quite crowded. The night illumination is only open on designated weekends in June, so if you want to avoid the crowds or capture serene shots, it's highly recommended to arrive on a weekday right when the garden opens, allowing about 1.5 to 2 hours to leisurely explore the expansive gardens and mountain paths.
三室戶寺.tagline: Stroll through Uji's vibrant hydrangea garden and discover enchanting heart-shaped blooms under weekend night lights.

長谷寺.types: over 40 varieties including Western hydrangeas and amethyst hydrangeas
長谷寺.fee: 大人 400円 (花期需另購紫陽花路入場券)
長谷寺.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Enoshima Electric Railway "Hase Station"
長谷寺.desc:
長谷寺 is the most famous hydrangea sanctuary in the Enoshima-Kamakura area. Along the hillside "Chobou Sansaku Road," over 2,500 hydrangeas of more than 40 varieties bloom. Every June, the path is flanked by cascading walls of colorful flowers, creating a dreamy hydrangea tunnel.

The most striking feature here is the blend of floral beauty and ocean views. As you ascend the hillside path, glimpses of the Yuigahama coastline and Kamakura streets peek through the vibrant flowers. Additionally, the charming "Ryouen Jizō" stone sculptures, each with a unique expression, hide among the blooms, making for popular photo spots.

During the peak bloom period in mid-June, the temple implements crowd control and issues "entry tickets (numbered passes)" due to the large number of visitors. It’s recommended to arrive early before the 8 AM opening to queue up, or check the official website for real-time wait times to avoid long waits on-site.
長谷寺.tagline: Wander through a vibrant tunnel of 40 flower varieties, with stunning views of the coast at this ancient temple.

豪斯登堡.address: 長崎県佐世保市ハウステンボス町1-1
豪斯登堡.access_train: Arrive at JR "Huis Ten Bosch Station"
豪斯登堡.access_car: About 10 minutes from the Nishi Kyushu Expressway "Oita IC"
豪斯登堡.desc:
At Huis Ten Bosch in Nagasaki, Kyushu, experience Japan's premier European-style hydrangea season. Nestled alongside canals and palaces reminiscent of the Netherlands, countless varieties of colorful hydrangeas bloom. Here, European castle architecture and exquisite gardening blend seamlessly, transporting you to a fairy-tale floral paradise.

A highlight of the park is the 200-meter-long "Hydrangea Avenue," lined with vibrant floral walls and clusters of blossoms. You can also take a "canal cruise" to enjoy the sprawling floral scenery from the water. As night falls, a stunning light show combines art and illumination, showcasing a breathtaking nighttime flower sea.

The Huis Ten Bosch park is vast, so it's recommended to plan for a full day. The best time for flower viewing and photography is from evening to the lighting show, allowing you to avoid the afternoon sun while capturing the sunset glow and the magical lights over the flowers. Be sure to check the official website for event announcements before your visit.
豪斯登堡.tagline: Step into a European canal-side hydrangea festival, where light art and vibrant floral walls create an enchanting celebration.

箱根登山電車.hours: 首班車至末班車（夜間點燈 18:30～22:00）
箱根登山電車.fee: 乘車券費用（大人單程約 460円）
箱根登山電車.address: 箱根登山鉄道沿線（箱根湯本站至強羅站）
箱根登山電車.access_train: Take the train from "Hakone Yumoto Station"
箱根登山電車.access_car: Driving is not recommended; taking the train is advised.
箱根登山電車.desc:
The Hakone Tozan Railway transforms into the "Hydrangea Train" from mid-June to early July, as approximately 10,000 hydrangeas bloom along the tracks. Passengers can enjoy a unique flower-viewing experience from the comfort of a train climbing the mountains, with vibrant flowers just a window away.

Due to the significant elevation changes along the railway, the flowers bloom gradually from lower to higher altitudes, extending the viewing season. During the flower season, many sections of the line are illuminated at night. The highlight is the limited-time, reserved-seating special train, "Yoru no Ajisai-go," which is extremely popular.

The "Yoru no Ajisai-go" operates only at night with special lighting and does not offer standing room, so it's essential to book tickets in advance through the official website, as it's challenging to find seats on-site. If you prefer to ride the regular train during the day, it's best to choose non-peak hours on weekdays and sit in the front or rear cars for the best views.
箱根登山電車.tagline: Ride the one and only "Hydrangea Train" and bask in the glow of illuminated blooms.

本土寺.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Joban Line "Kita-Kogane Station"
本土寺.access_car: About 20 minutes from Joban Expressway "Nagareyama IC"
本土寺.desc:
Hondoji, located in Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture, is renowned as the "Hydrangea Temple" of Kanto. With nearly 10,000 hydrangeas blooming during the rainy season in June, the colorful flowers beautifully complement the historical structures of the temple, including the Nio Gate, five-story pagoda, and main hall, radiating a deep sense of traditional Japanese Zen.

What makes this place truly unique is the opportunity to enjoy "double blooms." During the same flowering season, around 5,000 irises also reach their peak in the iris pond, creating a rich tapestry of colors with purple and white irises intertwined with the various hues of hydrangeas, resulting in a stunning early summer landscape.

Entry to Hondoji is free on regular days, but a "donation fee" is required during the June flower season. The pathways are mostly gentle gravel and stone steps, making for an easy stroll. Located just outside Tokyo, it’s a hidden gem for flower viewing, especially on weekday mornings when crowds are minimal.
本土寺.tagline: Discover a vibrant sea of hydrangeas and a classic five-story pagoda at this enchanting summer retreat.

楊谷寺.address: 京都府長岡京市浄土谷堂ノ谷2
楊谷寺.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Nagaokakyo Station or Hankyu Nagaokatenjin Station
楊谷寺.access_car: About 10 minutes from Kyoto Jukan Expressway Nagaokyo IC
楊谷寺.desc:
Yangakuziji, nestled in the mountains of Nagaokakyo, Kyoto, is the birthplace of 'Hanamizuki' in Japan. Every June during the flower season, the temple showcases stunning aesthetics by floating vibrant hydrangeas in the purification basin. Within its grounds, around 5,000 hydrangeas bloom beautifully among the ancient temple and bamboo groves.

The visual highlight of the park is the 'Ajisai Corridor' and the long 'Hydrangea Steps', flanked by potted plants and natural flower clusters, resembling a floral carpet. Additionally, the scenic 'Jodo-en' garden, visible from the main hall, and the seasonal exquisite 'Hanamizuki Goshuin' stamps are must-see attractions.

Located deep in the mountains, Yangakuziji attracts a large number of visitors during the flower season. The popular 'Upper Study Hall' is usually closed but opens for a limited time during the flower season for a fee, offering a breathtaking view of the garden. It's highly recommended to visit early on weekdays as soon as the gates open to capture the pristine scenery.
楊谷寺.tagline: Discover the birthplace of 'Hanamizuki' art amidst breathtaking hydrangea steps and ancient temple gardens.

雨引觀音.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Mito Line "Iwase Station"
雨引觀音.access_car: About 15 minutes from the North Kanto Expressway "Sakuragawa Chikusei IC"
雨引觀音.desc:
Yuhiki Kannon is an ancient mountain temple renowned for its prayers for children and safe childbirth. Within its grounds, approximately ten varieties and up to three thousand hydrangeas bloom each June, transforming this historic temple into a vibrant spectacle that beautifully merges ancient wooden architecture with nature's splendor.

The most popular attraction here is the late-season "Mizuchūka (floating hydrangeas in the pond)." The temple staff covers the entire pond with thousands of cut hydrangeas, creating a stunning, colorful carpet that floats on the water's surface, offering a truly dreamlike scene. Additionally, several peacocks roam freely around the temple, often posing alongside the blooming flowers.

The "Mizuchūka" floating flower carpet typically appears in early July and lasts for about one to two weeks, so be sure to check the official website for flower conditions and event schedules before your visit. Due to the steep steps at the temple, it's recommended to wear non-slip, comfortable shoes; visiting in the early morning not only means fewer crowds but also a better chance to see the peacocks.
雨引觀音.tagline: Experience the breathtaking floating hydrangea carpet and stroll with peacocks at this ancient temple of safe childbirth.

矢田寺.types: 60 varieties including Western hydrangeas, lacecap hydrangeas, and mountain hydrangeas
矢田寺.address: 奈良県大和郡山市矢田町3506
矢田寺.access_train: From Kintetsu Kōriyama Station / JR Hōryū-ji Station, take the Nara Kōtsū temporary shuttle bus for about 20 minutes
矢田寺.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Hōryū-ji IC on the Nishi-Meihan Expressway
矢田寺.desc:
Yatadera, officially known as Kongōsan-ji, was founded during the Hakuhō period and is one of Japan's oldest hydrangea spots. The changing colors of hydrangea petals resonate with Buddhist concepts of impermanence and the spirit of Kṣitigarbha, leading the temple to cultivate them extensively after the war. Today, the grounds boast around 60 varieties and nearly 10,000 hydrangeas, blooming from mid-May to early July.

The park's highlights include the diverse 'Ajisai Mibohon-en' and the 'Daimonbō Garden,' which offers stunning views of the flower-covered mountains. One of the most unique sights is the limited appearance of the 'Ajisai Ball,' where clusters of colorful blooms hang suspended in the air, making it a popular photo spot. Due to the many steps at the mountain temple, it's recommended for independent travelers to wear comfortable walking shoes and set aside about 2 hours for a leisurely stroll.

Yatadera is located in Yamato-Kōriyama City, Nara Prefecture. During the blooming season, visitors can take a temporary shuttle bus operated by Nara Kōtsū from Kintetsu Kōriyama Station or JR Hōryū-ji Station directly to the temple, with a travel time of about 20 minutes. The entrance fee during the blooming season is 500 yen, and the temple is usually open from 8:30 AM to 5 PM. As this is a popular destination, it's advisable to plan visits on weekday mornings to avoid the crowds.
矢田寺.tagline: Discover Japan's hydrangea haven, where vibrant blooms and ancient beliefs intertwine in a breathtaking temple setting.

桃源鄉岬.address: 宮崎県門川町遠見半島
桃源鄉岬.access_train: About 15 minutes by taxi from JR Kadogawa Station
桃源鄉岬.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Nobeoka Road's Kadogawa IC
桃源鄉岬.desc:
Located in Miyazaki's Kadogawa Town, 桃源鄉岬 is a hidden gem perched on the hills of the Hyuga-nada coast. Home to an impressive two million hydrangeas, this spot boasts one of Japan's largest flower displays. Every June, the vibrant sea of flowers intertwines with the azure Pacific Ocean and dramatic cliffs, creating a visually stunning spectacle.

What makes this place truly unique is the chance to enjoy a "dual bloom" experience. During the flowering season, alongside the hills adorned with hydrangeas, the precious "Aoba" trees also reach their peak bloom. The dreamy purple canopies of the Aoba trees harmonize beautifully with the colorful hydrangea sea below, creating a romantic color palette that is unparalleled in Japan.

Due to its remote coastal location and vast area, it's highly recommended to drive and allocate plenty of time for your visit. The coastal pathways offer little shade, and the early summer sun can be intense, so be sure to prepare for sun protection and hydration. The colors of the flower sea are most captivating when the morning light refracts off the ocean, making it the preferred time for seasoned photographers.
桃源鄉岬.tagline: Experience two million stunning blooms against a breathtaking coastal backdrop this early summer.

形原溫泉.address: 愛知県蒲郡市金平町一ノ沢28-1
形原溫泉.access_train: Direct bus from JR 'Gamagori Station' (during flower season)
形原溫泉.access_car: About 30 minutes from Tomei 'Otowa Gamagori IC'
形原溫泉.desc:
At 形原溫泉 'Ajisai no Sato' in Gamagori, Aichi Prefecture, you'll find one of the finest hydrangea spots in the Tokai region. Every June, around 50,000 hydrangeas bloom around the tranquil 'Botan no Ike', painting the lush green valley in vibrant colors, beautifully reflected in the calm waters of the lake.

The highlight here is the limited-time 'Ajisai Matsuri' night illumination. As dusk falls, carefully designed lights cast enchanting shadows on the flowers, creating a mysterious and romantic atmosphere. Even more special, in this well-preserved environment, you might catch a glimpse of wild 'Genji Hotaru' fireflies fluttering among the blooms.

The evenings during the flower festival are the busiest times. If you want to capture the magical moment of sunset over the lake and the first lights coming on, it's best to arrive around 4 or 5 PM. Additionally, the temperature can drop significantly in the valley at night, and mosquitoes can be a nuisance, so be sure to bring a light jacket and insect repellent.
形原溫泉.mapUrl: https://maps.google.com/?q=形原温泉+あじさいの里
形原溫泉.tagline: Experience a dreamlike festival with 50,000 blooming flowers and fireflies dancing under the night sky.

白山神社.access_train: 2-minute walk from Hakusan Station on the Toei Mita Line / 5-minute walk from Honkomagome Station on the Namboku Line
白山神社.access_car: Public transportation is recommended (no dedicated parking available)
白山神社.desc:
Hakusan Jinja, established in 948, is one of Tokyo's Ten Shrines, dedicated to the guardian of marriage and family harmony. The shrine, along with the adjacent Hakusan Park, is home to approximately 3,000 diverse hydrangeas. During the rainy season in June, the vibrant blooms beautifully complement the shrine's torii gates, stone paths, and historic buildings, making it one of the most iconic hydrangea spots in Tokyo.

The highlight of the flower season is undoubtedly the annual Bunkyo Hydrangea Festival held in early June. During this time, the normally closed "Fujizuka" will be specially opened, allowing visitors to ascend the path lined with hydrangeas. On weekends, there are concerts, traditional taiko performances, and lively outdoor stalls selling potted flowers and local delicacies, creating a vibrant atmosphere reminiscent of traditional Japanese festivals.

Located in Bunkyo, Tokyo, the shrine is easily accessible, just a 2-minute walk from Hakusan Station on the Toei Mita Line or a 5-minute walk from Honkomagome Station on the Namboku Line. Admission is free, and the grounds are open 24 hours (the office operates from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM). Due to the large crowds on festival weekends, it's highly recommended to visit on weekday mornings for a more relaxed flower viewing experience.
白山神社.tagline: Stroll through Tokyo's early summer wonder at the Bunkyo Hydrangea Festival, exploring ancient shrines and 3,000 blooming hydrangeas.

市民之森.types: Western hydrangeas, mountain hydrangeas, and more
市民之森.address: 北海道函館市上湯川町327-1
市民之森.access_train: Take a bus from Hakodate Station to '特拉普派修道院'
市民之森.desc:
Located in Hakodate, 市民之森 is Hokkaido's most iconic hydrangea haven, boasting around 13,000 blooming plants. Due to Hokkaido's cooler climate, the flowers bloom later than in Honshu, reaching their peak from late July to early August, making it an ideal summer destination for hydrangea lovers.

The park is adjacent to the famous European-style 特拉皮斯汀女子修道院, where lush forest trails beautifully contrast with the classic red-brick convent. This enchanting setting exudes a rich foreign charm. Additionally, the park's shop offers the renowned 'rich milk ice cream,' a classic treat that every visitor must try.

This flower season coincides with Hokkaido's peak travel period, and the area often sees a surge of tourists in the afternoon. It's recommended to plan your visit in the early morning or late morning to leisurely stroll through the dew-kissed hydrangeas and then visit the convent for a smooth and enjoyable experience.
市民之森.tagline: Explore Hokkaido's largest summer flower sea, framed by a European-style convent and must-try rich milk ice cream.

見歸瀑布.hours: 全天（花季期間夜間有燈光秀）
見歸瀑布.fee: 免費（環境維護協力金約 200円）
見歸瀑布.address: 佐賀県唐津市相知町伊岐佐
見歸瀑布.access_train: About 5 minutes by taxi from JR Chikuhi Line "Aizuchi Station"
見歸瀑布.access_car: About 20 minutes from Nagasaki Expressway "Taku IC"
見歸瀑布.desc:
Mikaeri Falls, located in Karatsu City, Saga Prefecture, is not only listed among the "Top 100 Waterfalls in Japan" but also stands out as one of Kyushu's most iconic hydrangea spots. Every year in late June, the magnificent waterfall, plunging over 100 meters, is surrounded by a stunning display of 40,000 hydrangeas blooming along the valley's streams, creating a breathtaking scene of harmony between strength and grace.

The highlight of the flower season is the natural filter of "mist and flower sea." The negative ions and fine mist generated by the cascading waterfall envelop the blooms, making the hydrangeas appear exceptionally vibrant. The suspension bridge spanning the valley offers the best vantage point to capture the flowing waterfall alongside the vibrant colors of the blooming flowers.

During the flower season in June, romantic lighting is implemented at night. Due to the abundant moisture from the waterfall, the surrounding trails and viewing platforms are often damp, so it's essential to wear good non-slip sports shoes when visiting. If you want to capture the stunning scenery of the waterfall and the flower sea together, it's recommended to bring a water-resistant phone or camera.
見歸瀑布.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Japan's top waterfalls amidst a sea of 40,000 hydrangeas and a scenic suspension bridge.

丹州觀音寺.types: 100 varieties including Western hydrangeas and Ajisai
丹州觀音寺.fee: 大人 500 円 (紫陽花季期間)
丹州觀音寺.address: 京都府福知山市観音寺1067
丹州觀音寺.access_car: About 5 minutes from Maizuru-Wakasa Expressway Fukuchiyama IC
丹州觀音寺.desc:
Tanshu Kannonji, located in Fukuchiyama City, Kyoto Prefecture, is renowned as Kansai's oldest "Hydrangea Temple." The temple boasts around 100 varieties and nearly 10,000 hydrangeas. Each June, the ancient temple becomes enveloped in a vibrant sea of colors, intertwining with the solemnity of its Buddhist halls, creating a profound Zen atmosphere.

What sets this place apart is its pure and serene temple flower-viewing experience. Unlike the bustling attractions in central Kyoto, this site retains the tranquility of a mountain temple. The flower clusters embrace ancient stone Buddhas, lanterns, and wooden structures, making a stroll here feel like a journey back in time. The temple also offers limited edition hydrangea charms during the blooming season, perfect for collectors.

Located in northern Kinki (Tamba region), Kannonji is away from Kyoto's city center, attracting fewer crowds, making it ideal for travelers seeking a high-quality, peaceful flower-viewing experience. The terrain can be uneven with stone steps during the blooming season, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. Allow about 1.5 hours to fully appreciate the Zen garden.
丹州觀音寺.tagline: Discover the tranquility of Kansai's oldest Hydrangea Temple, where a sea of blooms meets classic wooden architecture.

權現堂公園.address: 埼玉県幸手市大字内国府間887-3
權現堂公園.access_train: Take the Tobu Nikko Line to 'Satte Station' and transfer to a bus (toward Goka Town Hall), getting off at 'Gongen-do.'
權現堂公園.access_car: About 10 minutes from the Ken-O Expressway 'Satte IC.'
權現堂公園.desc:
Located in Saitama Prefecture's Satte City, Gongen-do Park (Gongen-do Dike) is famous for its cherry blossoms and rapeseed flowers in spring, transforming into a hydrangea paradise in early summer. Over 16,000 hydrangeas of more than 100 varieties, lovingly cultivated by local volunteers, bloom along the sprawling dike, creating a spectacular colorful floral carpet.

The park's most striking visual highlight is the 'Annabelle Hydrangea Valley,' composed of up to 3,000 pure white blooms. When this sea of white flowers fully blossoms on the hillside, it resembles a snow-capped mountain emerging in midsummer, offering a rare and magnificent sight not found in other flower viewing spots, making it a popular photo opportunity.

Gongen-do Dike features an open terrain with few tall trees for shade, and the midday sun in June can be intense. It is highly recommended to bring a sun umbrella and stay hydrated. The 'Yamato Tea House' along the dike sells local snacks, providing a perfect spot to rest while enjoying the flowers, especially in the soft light of dusk, which is ideal for photography.
權現堂公園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking sea of 16,000 blooms and the stunning white ‘Annabelle’ snow mountain at Gongen-do Park.

高幡不動尊.fee: 免費 (重要文化財參拜另計)
高幡不動尊.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Takahana Fudoson Station on the Keio Line and Tama Monorail
高幡不動尊.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Kunitachi-Fuchu IC on the Chuo Expressway
高幡不動尊.desc:
Takahana Fudoson (Kongō-ji) is a millennium-old temple located in Hino City, Tokyo, and is one of the three great Fudo temples of Kanto. Every year in June, the "Ajisai Matsuri" draws attention across the region, showcasing around 7,500 wild mountain hydrangeas and Western hydrangeas that beautifully complement the temple's ancient architecture nestled in the hillside.

The main visual highlight here is the striking red "Five-Story Pagoda" set against a backdrop of vibrant flowers. The flower viewing path also serves as a replica of the "Shikoku 88 Temples" pilgrimage route, dotted with countless stone Buddhas, allowing visitors to experience a cleansing spiritual aesthetic amidst the fragrant blossoms and towering ancient trees.

The flower-watching trail on the hillside features natural hilly terrain, with some steep and rocky sections, so sturdy hiking shoes are recommended. To avoid the crowds of pilgrims and flower enthusiasts, arriving before 8 AM is highly recommended for the most serene temple atmosphere.
高幡不動尊.tagline: Explore the ancient beauty of Kanto's three great Fudo temples, with a breathtaking pilgrimage through 7,500 blooming hydrangeas.

市民之森 (函館市民の森).tagline: Discover a northern oasis of late-blooming hydrangeas beside the Trappistine Monastery.
市民之森 (函館市民の森).desc:
Hakodate Shimin no Mori is adjacent to the famous Trappistine Monastery and boasts Hokkaido's largest hydrangea garden. Here, over 13,000 hydrangeas of around twenty varieties are carefully cultivated. Due to the cool climate, the flowers bloom late, peaking from late July to mid-August, painting the entire forest in vibrant hues.

The visual highlight is the serene interplay of towering trees and hydrangea walls. Under the expansive secondary forest, delicate blue and pure white flower bands stretch along the gravel paths, with dappled sunlight shimmering, creating an ethereal and pure beauty unique to the northern forests.

The park's gently rolling terrain and well-planned pathways make it perfect for leisurely strolls and hikes with the whole family. While enjoying the flowers, be sure to try the popular rich milk soft serve available at the entrance shop. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the least crowds, providing a cool and comfortable experience ideal for photography.
市民之森 (函館市民の森).address: 北海道函館市上湯川町327-1
市民之森 (函館市民の森).access_train: Take the JR train to Hakodate Station, then transfer to a Hakodate bus (toward Trappistine Monastery) and get off at 'Shimin no Mori Iriguchi', a 10-minute walk away.
市民之森 (函館市民の森).access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Hakodate Airport.

松前公園.address: 北海道松前郡松前町字松城
松前公園.access_train: About 90 minutes by Hakodate bus from JR Kaikyō Line "Kikonai Station"
松前公園.tagline: Experience a romantic summer night with 3,000 floating hydrangeas beneath the Edo Castle skyline.
松前公園.desc:
松前公園, located at the southernmost tip of Hokkaido, is renowned for the northernmost castle in Japan, 松前城. Due to its northern location, the hydrangeas here reach their peak bloom from mid-July to early August. The highlight of the park is the poetic "Water Hydrangea" event held in the recreated Bakumatsu streetscape of the 松前藩屋敷.

The most visually stunning feature is the "vibrant gradient covering the waterway." Approximately 3,000 hand-picked hydrangeas float densely in the waterways between the ancient wooden buildings. White, pink, and blue-purple blooms drift gracefully on the water's surface, intertwining with the stone-paved paths to create an elegant, classical Edo atmosphere.

Since 2021, the park has introduced a romantic lighting event during the flower season at night. The colorful floral carpet reflects a mysterious ambiance in the soft glow. As fresh flowers are continuously replaced throughout the event, visitors can capture the most dreamy scenes on any evening of the refreshing northern summer night.

中島公園.address: 北海道札幌市中央區中島公園1
中島公園.access_train: Direct access from Sapporo City Subway Namboku Line "Nakashima Park Station"
中島公園.tagline: Stroll through Sapporo's urban oasis and discover heart-shaped hydrangeas by the Iris Pond this summer.
中島公園.desc:
Nakashima Park is listed among the "Top 100 Urban Parks in Japan" and serves as the green lung adjacent to Sapporo's bustling Susukino district. Unlike the flower viewing during Japan's rainy season, the hydrangeas here bloom a month later due to the local climate, reaching their peak from mid-July to early August, adding vibrant early summer colors to Sapporo's brief summer.

The park's main visual highlight is the stunning contrast of "waterfront and flower fields." Around the expansive "Shobu Pond (Iris Pond)," pink, purple, and blue hydrangeas bloom along the riverside and shaded pathways. A hidden gem for seasoned travelers is the delightful challenge of finding the naturally formed "heart-shaped hydrangeas" amidst the colorful blooms.

Nakashima Park is open 24 hours for free and features flat terrain, making it perfect for leisurely walks. Due to the high temperatures during the midday summer, it is highly recommended to visit in the early morning or evening when the light is soft and the atmosphere is comfortable. This timing not only provides ideal conditions for photography but also allows for visits to the park's renowned Japanese garden, Hachiso-an, and Hoheikan.

豊平公園.address: 北海道札幌市豊平區豊平5条13丁目
豊平公園.access_train: Direct access from Sapporo City Subway Toho Line "Toyohira Koen Station" exit
豊平公園.tagline: Step into a botanical oasis and explore the vibrant hydrangea greenhouse this summer.
豊平公園.desc:
豊平公園 was once a forestry research site, now transformed into a plant paradise in Sapporo. The park's "Green Center" is a key highlight, featuring expansive outdoor lawns and gardens. Every year from early July to early August, the park bursts into color with its hydrangea season, showcasing pink, blue, and white blooms amidst lush greenery.

What sets this place apart is its exquisite blend of horticulture and landscape design. In addition to the outdoor hydrangea walking paths, the Green Center frequently hosts seasonal flower exhibitions and gardening workshops. The delicate art of bonsai and the blooming hydrangeas create a rich and educational flower-viewing experience for visitors.

The park features a large greenhouse, allowing for comfortable plant viewing regardless of the weather. During the flower season, consider combining a picnic on the outdoor lawn with the indoor exhibitions for a leisurely half-day itinerary. If you're looking to capture the essence of a Japanese garden, the interplay of coniferous trees and hydrangeas in the park offers perfect photo opportunities.

伊達市 有珠善光寺.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Muroran Line "Usuzan Station"
伊達市 有珠善光寺.access_car: About 15 minutes from Dō-ō Expressway "Date IC"
伊達市 有珠善光寺.tagline: Explore a millennium-old national treasure, surrounded by stunning hydrangeas in a serene wooden temple.
伊達市 有珠善光寺.desc:
Located in Date City, Usuzan Zenkoji is an ancient temple with over a thousand years of history, recognized as a national historic site in Japan. From mid-July to mid-August, around 1,000 hydrangeas bloom simultaneously within the temple grounds, beautifully contrasting with the solemn wooden main hall and treasure house, earning it the title of Hokkaido's most iconic "Hydrangea Temple."

The visual highlight here is the "fusion of historical heritage and nature." Amidst rustic stone lanterns, towering ancient trees, and lush moss, clusters of blue and purple blooms exude a deep sense of Zen. Additionally, the hydrangeas often frame the summer ema, showcasing a rich aesthetic of Japanese rituals.

The expansive grounds of Zenkoji feature natural walking paths shaded by trees, making summer strolls refreshingly cool. During the blooming season, it's recommended to set aside 1 to 1.5 hours for your visit. Don't forget to admire the ancient buildings, designated as cultural properties, especially on weekday mornings when you can capture serene and tranquil scenes away from the crowds.
伊達市 有珠善光寺.img: images/ajisai/伊達市 有珠善光寺/1.jpg

狩勝高原園地.address: 北海道上川郡新得町字新內651
狩勝高原園地.access_train: About 15 minutes from JR Nemuro Main Line's Shintoku Station
狩勝高原園地.access_car: Approximately 25 minutes from Tokachi Shimizu IC on the Doto Expressway
狩勝高原園地.tagline: Discover the breathtaking beauty of Tokachi's highlands, where early summer hydrangeas bloom in nature's embrace.
狩勝高原園地.desc:
Karakatsu Kogen Enchi, located in Shintoku, Hokkaido, is a leisure destination that beautifully blends stunning nature with highland scenery. Thanks to its altitude and unique climate, the hydrangea season here arrives earlier than in other parts of Hokkaido, typically from mid-June to early July, allowing visitors to witness a vibrant tapestry of flowers amidst the lush green mountains.

The highlight of this spot is the "vast landscapes and sea of flowers." Unlike the refined beauty found in temples, here, hydrangeas bloom freely across expansive highland grasslands and hills, set against the majestic Hidaka Mountains and the Tokachi Plain. The fresh, open air and the clusters of flowers scattered across the fields offer travelers a unique blend of Hokkaido's rugged charm and romance.

There are observation decks around the area that provide sweeping views of the entire flower sea and the distant plains. Due to its highland location, the early summer breeze can feel cool, so it's advisable to bring a lightweight windbreaker. This place is also a stargazing haven, so if time allows, visiting at dusk can let you capture the golden fields under the fading sunset.

せたな青少年旅行村.address: 北海道久遠郡せたな町瀨棚區西大里11
せたな青少年旅行村.access_train: Approximately 3 hours and 30 minutes by direct bus from Hakodate
せたな青少年旅行村.access_car: About 2 hours and 40 minutes from Hakodate city
せたな青少年旅行村.tagline: Experience breathtaking views of 30,000 hydrangeas against the stunning backdrop of the Japan Sea!
せたな青少年旅行村.desc:
Setana Youth Travel Village, located in Hokkaido's Kuon District, is a comprehensive outdoor leisure park perched on a high ridge. The most stunning spectacle here occurs in late July to mid-August, when the hillside overlooking the Japan Sea bursts into bloom with an impressive 30,000 hydrangeas, making it one of the top floral displays in the northern regions.

The ultimate visual treat is the "perfect harmony of unbeatable sea views and a sea of flowers." Stroll along the park's pathways, where towering walls of flowers surround you, and gaze into the distance to catch a glimpse of the iconic "Mitsumon Sugi Rock" and the azure Japan Sea. As the sun sets, the sea transforms into a golden hue, intertwining with the vibrant blooms on the hillside, creating a dreamlike scene.

The park features well-equipped camping areas and recreational facilities, making it an ideal spot for families to enjoy outdoor camping and flower viewing. Being a coastal highland, the sea breeze can be quite strong, so it's recommended to use a tripod for photography. The evening sunset is widely regarded as the best time for capturing stunning silhouette shots of the flower fields.
せたな青少年旅行村.displayName: 立象山公園（せたな青少年旅行村）

龍飛崎.address: 青森縣東津輕郡外ヶ濱町三厩龍濱
龍飛崎.access_train: About 30 minutes by bus from JR "Mikawa Station" to the town-operated bus in Togawaminato.
龍飛崎.tagline: Discover the breathtaking hydrangea path at the northernmost tip of the Tsugaru Peninsula, kissed by the sea breeze.
龍飛崎.desc:
Ryōhi-zaki, located at the northern tip of the Tsugaru Peninsula, is renowned for its strong sea winds. Due to its high latitude, the blooming season lasts from July to September. Every summer, the rugged coastline and cliffs are adorned with a sea of hydrangeas, adding a gentle splash of color to the magnificent, solitary seascape.

The visual highlight is the "Hydrangea Road," perfectly framed by the blue sky and ocean. Clusters of blue and purple flowers stretch along the road and pathways, with the majestic Ryōhi-zaki Lighthouse in the background. Additionally, the park features Japan's only "staircase national road," making it a must-visit spot for travelers seeking unique photo opportunities.

Being situated at the cape, even in midsummer, the sea breeze can be quite strong. It's advisable to bring a windbreaker and a hat with a strap for flower viewing and photography. There is no shelter here, so be sure to protect yourself from the sun; visiting in the early morning or late afternoon offers the most beautiful light for stunning landscape shots.

陸奧護國寺.address: 青森縣青森市淺虫山下203-6
陸奧護國寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Asamushi Onsen Station on the Aoimori Railway
陸奧護國寺.access_car: About 30 minutes from Aomori city center
陸奧護國寺.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Ajisai-dera, where ancient temples meet a vibrant sea of flowers this early summer.
陸奧護國寺.desc:
Rikuō Gokoku-ji, nestled in the Asamushi Onsen area, is Aomori's famous "Ajisai-dera". From late June to mid-July, around a thousand hydrangeas burst into bloom within the ancient temple grounds, adorning the solemn halls and tranquil paths with a riot of colors, exuding the profound Zen spirit of the northern lands.

The highlight here is the "classic harmony of ancient temple and blooming flowers". Vivid flower clusters embrace the ancient moss-covered stone steps, Jizō statues, and wooden structures. Especially on rainy days during the plum rain season, the petals glistening with droplets against the lush mountain backdrop appear exceptionally vibrant and poetic.

Being close to the hot spring district, it's perfect for planning a relaxing trip that combines soaking in the hot springs with flower viewing. The temple is built on a hillside, requiring a walk up stone steps, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. During the flower season, the crowds are relatively fewer than in the city, making weekday mornings the best time to enjoy the unique ethereal and tranquil atmosphere.

石川大仏公園.address: 青森縣弘前市大字石川大仏1
石川大仏公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Kōnan Railway "Ishikawa Station"
石川大仏公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Tōhoku Expressway "Owani Hirosaki IC"
石川大仏公園.tagline: Ascend the ancient castle ruins and immerse yourself in a sea of 2,500 hydrangeas at the Great Buddha's feet.
石川大仏公園.desc:
Ishikawa Daibutsu Park, located in Hirosaki City, is built on the site of the medieval "Ishikawa Castle" and named after the colossal "Ishikawa Daibutsu" that protects the area. From late June to mid-July, around 2,500 hydrangeas bloom in unison on the hillside, making it a beloved secret spot for locals.

The main highlights are the "Great Buddha, the sea of flowers, and the panoramic views." Follow the well-maintained paths to ascend, where vibrant blooms adorn the area around the Great Buddha, creating a scene reminiscent of paradise. At the summit observation deck, you can take in the entire floral carpet and enjoy stunning views of the majestic Iwaki Mountain.

The park's terrain is quite hilly, so wearing shoes with good grip is recommended. As there is little shade here, the midday summer heat can be intense, so it's best to visit in the early morning or late afternoon when the soft light is perfect for capturing rich landscapes.

館鼻公園.address: 青森縣八戶市湊町館鼻78-16
館鼻公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Hachinohe Line 'Mutsu Minato Station'
館鼻公園.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from JR 'Hachinohe Station'
館鼻公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking blend of hydrangeas and sea breeze at Hachinohe's coastal oasis.
館鼻公園.desc:
館鼻公園 is perched on a hill by Hachinohe Port. From late June to mid-July, vibrant clusters of hydrangeas bloom along the park's slopes and pathways, competing for attention against the lush greenery, all while kissed by the salty summer breeze.

The park's standout feature is the towering Guretto Tower Minato observation deck. Visitors can ascend for free and enjoy a 360-degree view of the azure Hachinohe Port, the Pacific Ocean, and a carpet of hydrangeas covering the hillside. The flowers and distant ships create a picturesque harbor town scene.

With excellent recreational facilities, the park is perfect for family outings. If you visit on a Sunday, be sure to start your day at the renowned 館鼻岸壁朝市, Japan's largest morning market, to savor delicious local treats, then take a leisurely stroll up the hill to admire the flowers for an authentic half-day experience in Hachinohe.

富士見湖パーク.address: 青森縣北津輕郡鶴田町廻堰大澤81-150
富士見湖パーク.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR "Mutsu-Tsuruta Station"
富士見湖パーク.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway "Owani IC"
富士見湖パーク.tagline: Stroll along Japan's longest wooden arch bridge and capture the stunning dialogue between Mt. Iwaki's reflection and blooming hydrangeas.
富士見湖パーク.desc:
富士見湖パーク is located by Lake Tsuruta in Tsugaru. From early July to late July, the shoreline bursts with colorful hydrangeas. This spot perfectly combines magnificent natural scenery with lakeside beauty, making it one of Aomori's most poetic flower-viewing secrets.

The most breathtaking sight is the "Tsuru no Mai Bridge framed by a sea of flowers." This is Japan's longest wooden triple-arch bridge, resembling a giant crane in flight. When the vibrant hydrangeas bloom on either side of the bridge, their colors intertwine with the distant reflection of Mt. Iwaki on the lake, creating a richly layered dreamlike scene.

The park's spacious and flat interior is perfect for leisurely walks. The best time for photography is during dusk, when the sunset casts a golden-red hue over the wooden bridge, the lake, and the hydrangeas, creating an incredibly romantic atmosphere. It can be breezy on the bridge, so it's advisable to bring a light jacket in summer.
富士見湖パーク.displayName: 富士見湖公園（鶴之舞橋）

八葉山天台寺.address: 岩手縣二戶市淨法寺町御山久保33
八葉山天台寺.access_train: Take the JR train to Ninohe Station, then transfer to a JR bus bound for Jōhōji for about 25 minutes, getting off at Tendaiji and walking for about 20 minutes.
八葉山天台寺.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes from the Jōhōji IC on the Hachinohe Expressway.
八葉山天台寺.tagline: Step into the serene world of Jikkyō Daishi at this ancient temple, where mossy stone steps meet ethereal hydrangeas.
八葉山天台寺.desc:
Tendaiji, located in Ninohe City, Iwate Prefecture, is a historic spiritual site renowned for its connection to the famous author Setouchi Jakuchō, who once served as its abbot. Every July, this ancient temple nestled in the mountains welcomes the hydrangea season, with delicate blue and purple blooms lining the mountain paths, earning it the title of the "Hydrangea Temple of the North."

The core visual experience here is the "historical feel of mossy stone steps and sea of flowers." Towering ancient trees and wild hydrangea clusters embrace both sides of the path, with rustic stone Buddhas subtly appearing among them. After the gentle rains of the plum rain season, petals adorned with droplets reflect the moss, showcasing a uniquely ethereal and Zen-like Japanese aesthetic.

As Tendaiji is built into the mountainside, reaching the main hall requires a climb up a long, somewhat steep gravel path. It is highly recommended to wear sturdy, grippy hiking shoes. Visiting during the flower season in the early morning allows you to enjoy the purest morning mist and tranquility of the forest.

川崎あじさい公園.address: 岩手縣一關市川崎町門崎渡戶218
川崎あじさい公園.access_train: About a 2-minute walk from JR Ōfunato Line "Rikuchū-Monozaki Station"
川崎あじさい公園.access_car: About 25 minutes from Tōhoku Expressway "Ichinoseki IC"
川崎あじさい公園.tagline: Step into a hidden early summer paradise, where thousands of hydrangeas bloom across rolling hills.
川崎あじさい公園.desc:
Kawasaki Ajisai Park is located in Kawasaki Town, Ichinoseki City, Iwate Prefecture. This cherished spot is lovingly maintained by local residents and the government, featuring thousands of diverse hydrangeas planted along the natural hilly terrain, making it one of the most representative early summer flower viewing destinations in southern Iwate Prefecture.

The visual highlight is the "colorful three-dimensional flower carpet." As you stroll along the park's well-planned walking paths, you'll see pink, sky blue, and deep purple flower clusters interwoven on the lush green slopes. The gently sloping lawn area is surrounded by flower walls, making it perfect for capturing layered floral scenes in photographs.

Unlike the steep trails of ancient mountain temples, this park offers relatively flat and well-maintained paths, making it ideal for families with elders or children to enjoy a leisurely walk. On sunny summer days, the park has little shade, and the midday sun can be intense, so it's advisable to bring an umbrella and stay hydrated. Visiting before dusk provides the softest lighting for your experience.

秋保大滝植物園.fee: 大人 240円 / 中學生以下 免費
秋保大滝植物園.address: 宮城縣仙台市太白區秋保町馬場大滝5
秋保大滝植物園.access_train: About 90 minutes from JR Sendai Station via city bus (toward Akihō Ōtaki)
秋保大滝植物園.access_car: About 30 minutes from Sendai Minami IC on the Tohoku Expressway
秋保大滝植物園.tagline: Experience Japan's majestic waterfalls and discover vibrant hydrangeas in the heart of nature.
秋保大滝植物園.desc:
The botanical garden is nestled next to the famous Akihō Ōtaki waterfall. From early June to late July, the trails alongside the mountains and waters burst with elegant hues of mountain hydrangeas, harmonizing with the thunderous roar of the distant waterfall, showcasing a lively valley of flowers.

What sets this place apart is the stunning visual of "pristine forest meeting a sea of flowers." Unlike manicured gardens, the blooms intertwine naturally among dense foliage and moss-covered rocks, filling the air with a high concentration of negative ions. The blue flower clusters, adorned with droplets, stand out vividly against the lush green valley.

The walking paths are built along the natural terrain, with some sections featuring gentle slopes and gravel. It’s recommended to wear good hiking shoes with slip-resistant soles. While enjoying the flowers, you can also take a detour to the nearby waterfall observation deck for a breathtaking view, making it an ideal starting point for a rejuvenating day trip to Akihō Onsen.

國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.fee: 大人 450円 / 65歲以上 210円 / 中學生以下 免費
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.address: 宮城縣柴田郡川崎町小野二本松53-9
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.access_train: About 60 minutes by bus from JR Sendai Station to Akiu/Kawasaki Sendai Seibu Line
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.access_car: About 5 minutes from Miyagi Kawasaki IC on the Yamagata Expressway
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.tagline: Stroll through vibrant flower fields by Lake Kamafusa and discover Tohoku's charming thatched-roof cottages.
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.desc:
Nestled along the shores of Lake Kamafusa, this expansive national park is renowned for its picturesque seasonal flower displays. From late June to mid-July, the hydrangea garden reaches its peak, with over ten thousand blooms of dozens of rare varieties gracefully unfolding across the lush landscape, transforming the entire park into a colorful spectacle.

The main visual highlight is the "traditional thatched-roof cottages amidst the floral splendor." The park features replicas of old thatched-roof houses from various regions of Tohoku, and when surrounded by vibrant clusters of hydrangeas, these quaint wooden structures and stone lanterns evoke a nostalgic scene straight out of Japan's ancient folklore.

Given the park's vast expanse, it's highly recommended to rent a bicycle or take the park's small train for easy navigation. With little shade available, be sure to prepare for sun protection and mosquito repellent during the summer flower viewing. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the perfect time for a leisurely stroll and capturing breathtaking photos.
國營みちのく杜の湖畔公園.displayName: 國營みちのく湖畔公園

加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.address: 宮城縣大崎市田尻大澤加護峰山178-1
加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.access_train: About 10 minutes from JR Tohoku Main Line "Tajiri Station"
加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.access_car: About 30 minutes from Tohoku Expressway "Furukawa IC"
加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.tagline: Ascend the mountain path, embracing the breathtaking blend of the Osaki Plain and blooming flowers.
加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.desc:
Kagoboyama is renowned as a hiking paradise with 360-degree panoramic views. The "Tajiri Ajisai Road" stretches along both sides of the mountain path, adorned with hydrangeas. From late June to mid-July, thousands of hydrangeas burst into bloom alongside the winding road, creating a stunning floral highway with graceful lines.

The visual highlight is the immersive experience of driving or strolling through clusters of vibrant flowers. The blue and purple flower walls rise and fall with the road's incline, resembling a colorful ribbon wrapping around the lush mountains. Upon reaching the summit at 224 meters, you can take in the sprawling floral landscape alongside the magnificent Osaki Plain and distant mountain ranges.

This location features an open mountain road, so whether you're driving or stopping for photos, please stay aware of oncoming traffic for safety. The summit offers a spacious lawn and a scenic restaurant, making it highly recommended to visit on a clear morning for the best views of the plateau and hydrangeas.
加護坊山 田尻あじさいロード.displayName: 加護坊山・田尻繡球花路

船岡城址公園.address: 宮城縣柴田郡柴田町大字船岡舘山95-1
船岡城址公園.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Tōhoku Main Line "Funako Station"
船岡城址公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Tōhoku Expressway "Shiroishi IC"
船岡城址公園.tagline: Ride a vintage monorail through a historic castle and a tunnel of blooming flowers.
船岡城址公園.desc:
Funakawajōshi Park is one of Japan's top cherry blossom spots, but from mid-June to early July, it transforms into a vibrant hydrangea hill. The park is home to thousands of hydrangeas, with colorful blooms cascading along the slopes of ancient castle ruins and remnants of old city walls.

The most iconic experience here is the "Hydrangea Slope Car." Visitors can take a leisurely ride on a vintage monorail, gliding up the hill with flower walls towering above, immersing themselves in a rare railway aesthetic that weaves through a tunnel of blossoms.

The monorail is extremely popular during the blooming season, often requiring a wait on weekends, so it's best to head there right when the park opens. At the summit, you can admire the impressive Funakawa Heiwa Kannon statue and take in views of Shibata Town and the Shiroishi River. The mountaintop can be windy with little shade, so it's advisable to bring a sun umbrella and a light jacket.

野草園（仙台市）.fee: 大人 240円 / 小中學生 60円
野草園（仙台市）.address: 宮城縣仙台市太白區茂ヶ崎2-1-1
野草園（仙台市）.access_train: Take a city bus from JR Sendai Station directly to Yasoen.
野草園（仙台市）.tagline: Discover a hidden city forest with ethereal trails of native hydrangeas in the heart of Sendai.
野草園（仙台市）.desc:
Sendai's Yasoen, nestled within Dainichiji Mountain, is a treasure trove dedicated to preserving and showcasing the region's wild flora. From late June to mid-July, the expansive forest blooms with untouched native mountain hydrangeas, presenting a rustic yet elegant early summer charm.

The core experience revolves around the "intertwining of natural wooden pathways and wildflower clusters." The park features wooden trails designed to harmonize with nature, allowing visitors to stroll amidst delicate water-blue blooms hidden among towering ancient trees and lush moss. In contrast to the hustle and bustle of typical attractions, this place exudes an unparalleled sense of tranquility and ethereality.

As the park aims to protect the wild ecosystem, some forest paths are quite rustic and covered with fallen leaves. It is highly recommended to wear comfortable walking shoes and bring mosquito repellent for flower viewing. The serene environment is perfect for travelers who enjoy a leisurely pace and plant ecology photography, especially on quiet weekday mornings.

折渡あじさいロード.address: 秋田縣由利本荘市岩谷麓折渡
折渡あじさいロード.access_train: A short walk from JR Uetsu Main Line 'Oridato Station' (about 2 kilometers along the route)
折渡あじさいロード.access_car: About 10 minutes from the Nihonkai Tohoku Expressway 'Ouchi IC'
折渡あじさいロード.tagline: A two-kilometer journey through a sea of flowers and windmills awaits you this early summer.
折渡あじさいロード.desc:
The Oridato Ajisai Road is located at the Oridato Pass in Yurihonjo City. Along both sides of the prefectural road, approximately 4,000 hydrangeas bloom over a stretch of about two kilometers. In early July, clusters of blue and purple flowers blossom along the winding mountain path, bringing vibrant life to this ancient forest route, making it one of Akita's famous scenic roads.

The core visual experience here is the harmony between the blooming flowers and the sacred spiritual site. The trail is dotted with the historic Oridato Senju Kannon and numerous Jizo stone sculptures, with colorful flower clusters embracing the rustic statues. On clear days, you can capture a unique contrast between the distant giant wind turbines and the sea of flowers.

This area features an open mountain road, with some sections lacking sidewalks, so please be cautious of oncoming traffic while driving slowly or stopping for photos. The deep mountain area often experiences fog in the mornings, adding a mystical beauty to the flower fields and stone Buddhas, making it highly recommended for travelers who love cultural photography.

翠雲公園.address: 秋田縣北秋田市三木田173-186
翠雲公園.access_train: About 5 minutes by taxi from Akita Inland Line "Aikawa Station"
翠雲公園.tagline: Discover the vibrant floral tapestry and breathtaking peaks of Morioka at Suiun Park!
翠雲公園.desc:
Suiun Park, located in the Mikita area of Kita Akita City, is a beautifully landscaped park built around natural hills. From mid-July to early August each year, around 3,000 hydrangeas reach their peak bloom, creating a stunning display of colorful flower balls that blanket the slopes, making it one of the most iconic summer spots in Kita Akita.

The park's main visual highlight is the "360-degree floral carpet and panoramic highland views." As you stroll up the hillside paths, the blue and purple sea of flowers spreads out beneath your feet; from the top, you can take in the majestic peaks of Morioka and the pastoral scenery of the Takanosu basin, offering an expansive view.

The park features grassy slopes and steps, so it's highly recommended to wear comfortable, slip-resistant shoes for flower viewing. Being situated on high ground, the midday summer sun can be quite glaring and there is little shade, so visiting during the evening is suggested. The sunset glow illuminating the flower fields and the night view of the basin creates a truly romantic atmosphere.

出塩文殊堂（あじさい寺）.tagline: Stroll through 500 meters of hydrangea-lined path and discover the serene beauty of ancient temples in the rainy season.
出塩文殊堂（あじさい寺）.desc:
出塩文殊堂 is Yamagata's most iconic "Hydrangea Temple." From the entrance of the path to the main hall, around 2,500 hydrangeas bloom. Each year, from late June to early July, the 500-meter classical approach transforms into a deep blue and purple sea of flowers, beautifully contrasting with the ancient wooden temple architecture, creating a truly zen atmosphere.

The main visual focus here is the "moss-covered stone steps framed by vibrant blooms." As you ascend the stone path, the lush flower clusters nearly obscure the walkway, exuding an ethereal and mysterious ambiance. Especially on rainy days during the monsoon season, the water-drenched petals appear even more vivid against the lush green forest backdrop.

Since the path consists of natural stone steps, it can be slippery when wet, so wearing non-slip casual or athletic shoes is highly recommended. This tranquil environment is perfect for travelers who enjoy a slow pace and cultural photography, with weekday mornings being the best time to avoid crowds.
出塩文殊堂（あじさい寺）.access_train: Take a bus from JR Yamagata Station for about 20 minutes to "Shide" and walk about 10 minutes.
出塩文殊堂（あじさい寺）.access_car: About 15 minutes from the Tohoku Chuo Expressway "Yamagata Chuo IC."

笹野観音.tagline: Step into the ancient serenity of Sasano Kannon, where history and early summer blooms intertwine.
笹野観音.desc:
Sasano Kannon is an ancient spiritual site in Yonezawa City with over a thousand years of history, renowned as the birthplace of the local traditional craft, Sasano Ittobori. Each early summer, from late June to early July, the solemn temple grounds are enveloped in vibrant clusters of hydrangeas, adding a soft touch of color to this historic sanctuary.

The visual allure here lies in the nostalgic dialogue between the "massive thatched roof and the sea of flowers." The well-preserved main hall boasts a grand thatched roof, and when the vivid blue-purple flower clusters frame the rustic wooden structures and stone lanterns, it radiates a distinctly Tohoku charm and sacred atmosphere.

The terrain around the worship hall is flat and easy to navigate, making it perfect for a leisurely stroll. While visiting, it's highly recommended to take the opportunity to appreciate or experience the local woodcraft (such as the famous Sasano toy, "Otaka Poppo"). Mornings on weekdays see fewer worshippers, making it the ideal time for private photography sessions.
笹野観音.address: 山形県米沢市笹野本町5686-5
笹野観音.access_train: About 20 minutes by city bus (southbound route) from JR Yonezawa Station
笹野観音.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Yonezawa Kita IC on the Tohoku Chuo Expressway

最上公園（あじさい園）.tagline: Experience the stunning reflection of history and vibrant hydrangeas by the ancient castle's moat.
最上公園（あじさい園）.desc:
Mogami Park is a historical park built on the ruins of "Shinjo Castle" from the Edo period, and it’s a renowned floral oasis in Shinjo City. From mid-June to early July, the hydrangea garden surrounding the ancient castle site reaches its peak, with thousands of hydrangeas blooming along the moat, painting the lush park in a riot of colors.

The core feature here is the "mirror-like interplay between the moat's waters and the sea of flowers." The vibrant blooms embrace the old stone walls and remnants of the castle, and when the calm water perfectly reflects the clusters of blue and purple flowers alongside the distant Torii gate of Tenman-gu Shrine, the scene becomes incredibly rich and is a classic spot beloved by photography enthusiasts.

The park's flat terrain and well-planned pathways make it ideal for families to stroll and enjoy the flowers together. Since this area combines historical castle ruins with shrine landscapes, it’s recommended to set aside an hour to savor the experience. Early morning is the best time to capture the perfect reflection of the blooming flowers on the tranquil surface of the moat.
最上公園（あじさい園）.access_car: About 10 minutes from Tohoku Chuo Expressway "Shinjo IC"

東山公園（あじさいの杜）.tagline: Experience a breathtaking tapestry of 45,000 hydrangeas in the stunning Ajisai no Mori of Higashiyama Park.
東山公園（あじさいの杜）.desc:
Ajisai no Mori in Higashiyama Park is a premier hydrangea haven in Yamagata Prefecture. Nestled on the expansive natural hillsides, it boasts over 30 varieties of approximately 45,000 hydrangeas. During the peak bloom in July, the entire valley is blanketed in a vibrant sea of flowers, creating a truly striking visual spectacle.

The main highlight here is the "mountainous expanse of colorful ribbons." As you stroll along the winding pathways, you'll find yourself surrounded by blooms taller than you. From the elevated viewpoints, you can take in the endless blue and purple floral tapestry, showcasing the majestic beauty unique to the mountainous landscape.

Due to the park's vast area and hilly terrain, flower viewing involves climbing uphill paths. It is highly recommended to wear supportive and non-slip hiking shoes. Be prepared for summer insects and sun exposure, and allow 1.5 to 2 hours to fully enjoy the experience.
東山公園（あじさいの杜）.access_train: About 5 minutes from JR Shinjō Station
東山公園（あじさいの杜）.access_car: About 10 minutes from Tōhoku Chūō Expressway Shinjō IC

花咲山展望台.tagline: Experience romance atop a scenic peak, overlooking the enchanting hydrangea trails of Kamiyama Onsen.
花咲山展望台.desc:
Hanazakiyama Observation Deck, located in the Hayama area of Kamiyama, is renowned for its 360-degree panoramic views and is certified as a "Lovers' Sanctuary." From late June to mid-July, the trails leading to the summit are lined with vibrant hydrangeas, adding a splash of early summer color to this romantic high point.

The most breathtaking experience awaits as you "ring the bell of happiness while gazing at the Zao mountain range." At the observation deck, you can take in the stunning views of Kamiyama Onsen, Kamiyama Castle, and the majestic Zao mountains in the distance, all while surrounded by blooming hydrangeas and the happiness bell—a perfect spot for couples and Instagram enthusiasts alike.

To reach the observation deck, you'll need to walk along a hilly trail, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. This area has little shade, and the midday summer sun can be intense, so visiting at dusk is highly recommended. Not only will you enjoy a cooler atmosphere, but you'll also capture the golden sea of flowers and the charming night view of the onsen town bathed in the glow of the setting sun.
花咲山展望台.access_train: About 5 minutes by train from JR Kamiyama Onsen Station, followed by a walk uphill
花咲山展望台.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from Tohoku Chuo Expressway Kamiyama IC

霞ヶ城公園.tagline: Experience the breathtaking blend of history and early summer hydrangeas at Nihonmatsu Castle ruins.
霞ヶ城公園.desc:
Kasumiga-jo Park, located at the site of Nihonmatsu Castle, is a county park where nature and history intertwine. From late June to mid-July, the park is not only famous for its cherry blossoms and autumn chrysanthemums, but also for the hydrangeas blooming quietly around the Oishi-gaki and Shonen-tai Hill, adding vibrant colors to the majestic ancient castle walls.

The visual highlight here is the harmonious contrast of the historical stone walls and blooming flowers. As you stroll along the walking paths, you can see the delicate blue and purple flower clusters beautifully complementing the stone walls that date back to the Edo period. After the rainy season, the petals glistening with raindrops stand out even more against the robust backdrop of the ancient castle walls.

The park is spacious and situated in a mountainous area, with some paths having slight inclines, so comfortable walking shoes are highly recommended for flower viewing. During the blooming season, the crowds are relatively sparse, making early morning visits ideal for capturing the ethereal beauty of the ancient castle scenery, along with a chance to explore the park's Washin-tei and Futo-ike.
霞ヶ城公園.address: 福島県二本松市郭内3丁目
霞ヶ城公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Tohoku Main Line 'Nihonmatsu Station'
霞ヶ城公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Tohoku Expressway 'Nihonmatsu IC'

白河関の森公園.tagline: Explore ancient border history and discover a sea of blooms, including the trending 'Heart-Shaped Hydrangea' secret spot.
白河関の森公園.desc:
Shirakawa Seki no Mori Park is located next to one of Japan's three ancient checkpoints, the 'Shirakawa Seki Ato,' and is a nature park brimming with historical charm. From late June to mid-July, around 1,700 hydrangeas reach their peak bloom, adorning the hillsides and lawns with vibrant colors and life.

Don't miss the visual delight of searching for the limited 'Heart-Shaped Hydrangea.' The park features both Western hydrangeas and the pure white variety 'Annabelle,' where you might discover naturally formed heart-shaped flower clusters among the lush blooms—an increasingly popular photo spot for couples and influencers in recent years.

The park also offers a vintage thatched-roof house, playgrounds, and craft experience centers, making it perfect for a family half-day outing. Due to the intense early summer sun at this highland location, it's advisable to bring sunscreen and visit on weekday mornings for the most relaxed flower viewing experience and ideal photo lighting.
白河関の森公園.address: 福島県白河市旗宿白河内7-2
白河関の森公園.access_train: Take a bus for about 30 minutes from JR 'Shirakawa Station' to 'Shirakawa Seki.'
白河関の森公園.access_car: About 30 minutes from the Tohoku Expressway 'Shirakawa IC.'

亀ヶ城公園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking tapestry of blue hydrangeas at the historic foot of Bandai Mountain.
亀ヶ城公園.desc:
Kamega Castle Park, built around the historic sites of Inawashiro Castle and Tsurugamine Castle, offers stunning views of Mount Bandai. From late June to mid-July, the walking paths on the eastern side of the park and the northern area known as 'Ajisai Yurin no Sato' bloom with about 500 Western hydrangeas, creating a picturesque scene.

The park's standout feature is the 'blue flower carpet that spreads along the hillside.' Stroll along the naturally sloped walking paths, flanked by clusters of water blue, pink, and deep purple blooms. Some rare varieties even showcase a stunning gradient of colors on the same plant, beautifully contrasting with the lush green hills of the ancient castle.

As the main hydrangea area is located along steep walking paths, please be cautious of your footing while enjoying the flowers and taking photos. It is highly recommended to wear shoes with good grip. The park sees relatively few visitors, making it perfect for independent travelers seeking a quality experience away from the crowds.
亀ヶ城公園.address: 福島県耶麻郡猪苗代町古城跡7150-1
亀ヶ城公園.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Ban'etsu West Line 'Inawashiro Station'
亀ヶ城公園.access_car: About 10 minutes from Inawashiro Bandai Kogen IC on the Ban'etsu Expressway

波立薬師（波立寺）.tagline: Discover the breathtaking 'Hydrangea Temple' where vibrant blooms meet the stunning red bridge and azure sea.
波立薬師（波立寺）.desc:
波立薬師 (Habatari Yakushi) is an ancient Rinzai Zen temple dedicated to maritime safety, affectionately known as 'Hydrangea Temple' due to its abundant hydrangea blooms. Each June, thousands of colorful flowers burst into bloom among the ancient halls and lush forests, weaving a prelude to early summer with the salty sea breeze.

The most striking visual highlight is the expansive view of 波立海岸 (Habatari Coast) across the road. Visitors can capture the vibrant flower fields beside the ancient temple, along with the iconic red bridge and the prominent 弁天島 (Benten Island) against the backdrop of the blue sky and sea, creating a rare blend of beauty found nowhere else in Japan.

The coast is uniquely composed of round pebbles, and local lore warns that taking these stones may lead to eye ailments, so please refrain from picking them up during your visit. It is recommended to visit on a clear morning or just before dusk, when the morning light or the warm glow of sunset reflects off the sea and flowers, creating the most stunning photographic opportunities.
波立薬師（波立寺）.types: European hydrangeas (mainly in shades of blue and purple)
波立薬師（波立寺）.address: 福島県いわき市久之浜町田之網横内89
波立薬師（波立寺）.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR 常磐線 (Joban Line) 久之浜駅 (Kuzuhama Station)
波立薬師（波立寺）.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from 常磐自動車道 (Joban Expressway) いわき四倉IC (Iwaki Yotsukura IC)

ジュピアランドひらた.tagline: Experience the breathtaking beauty of Hirata Village's world-record hydrangea display with 28,000 vibrant blooms.
ジュピアランドひらた.desc:
Located in Hirata Village, ジュピアランドひらた is a world-class hydrangea destination that captivates all of Japan. Spanning an impressive 50,000 square meters, it boasts 825 varieties and approximately 28,000 hydrangeas, having set a Guinness World Record for the "most varieties of hydrangeas on display."

The highlight is the chance to see rare and original varieties from around the globe. The expansive "World Hydrangea Garden" features not only globally renowned types but also exclusive new varieties cultivated in Hirata Village. During early July, the breathtaking, multi-colored floral carpet creates a stunning visual impact.

During the blooming season, an admission fee is charged (800 yen for high school students and above), and on select weekends, a special early opening at 6 AM is offered. We highly recommend visiting during the cool, quiet mornings to enjoy a peaceful stroll and capture stunning photos of the dew-kissed, sprawling flower fields.
ジュピアランドひらた.types: the most varieties in the world (about 825)
ジュピアランドひらた.address: 福島県石川郡平田村蓬田新田蓬田岳437
ジュピアランドひらた.access_train: About 45 minutes by taxi from JR "Koriyama Station"
ジュピアランドひらた.access_car: About 5 minutes from the あぶくま高原道路 "Hirata IC"

雨引觀音 (樂法寺).tagline: Experience the breathtaking 'Floating Hydrangea' spectacle amidst peacocks and a millennia-old temple.
雨引觀音 (樂法寺).desc:
雨引觀音 is a millennia-old temple renowned for prayers for safe childbirth, and it's one of Kanto's top hydrangea spots. From mid-June to early July, around ten varieties and up to three thousand hydrangeas bloom exuberantly, adorning the solemn wooden Buddha hall and stone steps with vibrant colors.

The temple is famous for its stunning late-season display known as 'Floating Hydrangea.' During this time, thousands of colorful blooms are carefully placed on the pond, creating a dreamlike, multicolored flower carpet that floats on the water's surface. Additionally, the sight of peacocks wandering freely among the flowers is a rare and precious experience.

The 'Floating Hydrangea' flower carpet typically debuts in early July and lasts for about one to two weeks, so be sure to check the official website for the latest schedule before your visit. The temple's steep steps can be challenging, so wearing shoes with good grip is recommended. Arriving in the early morning not only means fewer crowds but also increases your chances of encountering peacocks up close.
雨引觀音 (樂法寺).types: Floating hydrangeas and about 100 other varieties
雨引觀音 (樂法寺).access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Mito Line 'Iwase Station'
雨引觀音 (樂法寺).access_car: About 15 minutes from the Kitakanto Expressway 'Sakuragawa Chikusei IC'

国營ひたち海浜公園.tagline: Step into a hidden early summer paradise of hydrangeas, swaying in the dappled light of pure white Annabelle blooms.
国營ひたち海浜公園.desc:
Kokuei Hitachi Seaside Park is famous for its spring cosmos and autumn pampas grass, but from mid-June to early July, the hydrangea season in the "Hina no Mori" area is equally enchanting. Here, the pure white variety "Annabelle," native to North America, creates a dreamy white snow world among towering pine trees.

The park's biggest feature is the "dialogue between dappled light and the sea of pure white flowers." As the blooming period progresses, the Annabelle transitions from pale green to pure white. On sunny days, sunlight filters through the pine forest, casting a shimmering glow on the white and green flower clusters, creating a scene that feels like a fairy tale.

The park is vast, and the hydrangea area is located in a shaded section, making it cooler compared to the flower fields under the blazing sun. Renting a bicycle at the entrance is recommended for easy exploration. On rainy days, the contrast between the lush green leaves and the pure white flower clusters is even more striking, making it a perfect spot for eco-photography enthusiasts, regardless of the weather.
国營ひたち海浜公園.address: 茨城県ひたちなか市馬渡605-4
国營ひたち海浜公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR "Katsuta Station"
国營ひたち海浜公園.access_car: About 1 minute from the Hitachinaka Toll Road "Hitachi Seaside Park IC"

大宝八幡宮.tagline: Stroll through Kanto's oldest Hachiman shrine, where 30,000 vibrant hydrangeas bloom on ancient castle mounds.
大宝八幡宮.desc:
Daihō Hachiman-gū was established in 701 AD, making it the oldest Hachiman shrine in Kanto and a renowned site for wishing for lottery success. From mid-June to early July, the shrine cultivates around 4,000 hydrangeas of approximately 300 varieties on the slopes of the ancient castle ruins.

The visual highlight is the three-dimensional floral display created by the elevation changes of the mounds. Visitors can walk into the unguarded Ajisai Shinen, where they can gaze up from below or look down from above at the stunning floral wall, showcasing a unique gradient of colors in nature's palette. At the end of the blooming season, the shrine also distributes cuttings for free.

The garden is open 24 hours a day, but there is no nighttime lighting, so visiting during the day is highly recommended. On Sundays in late June, a spectacular Yabusame Shinto event (horseback archery) takes place, with free barley tea available on-site, making it a perfect opportunity to experience a traditional Japanese festival.
大宝八幡宮.access_train: About a 3-minute walk from Kanto Railway Jōsō Line 'Daihō Station'
大宝八幡宮.access_car: About 25 minutes from the Jōban Expressway 'Jōsō IC'

北茨城あじさいの森.tagline: Explore Japan's largest private hydrangea forest, where 1,500 rare varieties create a breathtaking floral tapestry.
北茨城あじさいの森.desc:
北茨城あじさいの森 is a top-tier hydrangea garden cultivated over many years by local collector Mr. Yamagata. Nestled within a vast 30,000 square meter cedar forest, it boasts an impressive 1,500 varieties and around 30,000 hydrangea plants, making it the richest collection in Japan.

The main attraction is the opportunity to "step into a world-class hydrangea encyclopedia." The garden features a walking path lined with Yamazaki hydrangeas and a tunnel of Annabelle white flowers, along with the introduction of the latest European varieties in recent years. Climbing up to "Observation Hill" offers a breathtaking view of a vibrant floral carpet that stretches as far as the eye can see, delivering a stunning visual experience.

During the blooming season (mid-June to mid-July), a 500 yen entrance fee is charged for areas with over 50% bloom. Due to the natural undulations of the cedar forest, some paths are narrow dirt trails, so sturdy hiking shoes with good grip are highly recommended, along with mosquito repellent.
北茨城あじさいの森.types: 1,200 varieties including native and improved foreign species
北茨城あじさいの森.address: 茨城県北茨城市華川町小豆畑1138
北茨城あじさいの森.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Joban Line "Isohara Station"
北茨城あじさいの森.access_car: About 10 minutes from Kita-Ibaraki IC on the Joban Expressway

水戸八幡宮.tagline: Step into a serene world of hydrangeas at Mito Hachiman-gu, where nature's beauty unfolds in vibrant colors.
水戸八幡宮.desc:
Mito Hachiman-gu is an ancient shrine featuring a nationally designated important cultural property, and it’s one of Mito City’s prime flower viewing spots in early summer. From late May to early July, around 5,000 hydrangeas of 60 different varieties bloom sequentially on a small hill within the shrine grounds.

Don’t miss the enchanting "Yama Ajisai no Komichi" (Path of Mountain Hydrangeas). Under the shade of towering cedar trees, delicate native mountain hydrangeas stretch along the approach and gravel paths like a colorful ribbon, showcasing a rustic, elegant, and sacred Zen aesthetic.
