Suizenji Etsuko Park is a sprawling urban oasis built around the massive natural spring lake, Etsuko, in Kumamoto City. Every June, thousands of hydrangeas burst into bloom along the gentle waterfront paths and clear streams, creating a romantic corridor of coolness.

The park's highlight is the interplay of "clear springs, open grassy fields, and colorful flower walls." Strips of blue and purple flowers bloom alongside the tranquil river and gravel paths, harmonizing with the blue sky and distant towering ancient trees, showcasing the freshness and purity unique to Kumamoto's nature.

The park's pathways are fully renovated and flat, making it perfect for strollers and leisurely joggers. It is open to the public free of charge all day. Due to limited shade in the surrounding area, the midday summer sun can be intense, so it's highly recommended to bring sun protection and stay hydrated while enjoying the flowers and taking photos. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays offers the most refreshing experience.
水前寺江津湖公園.address: 熊本県熊本市東区湖東
水前寺江津湖公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from Kumamoto City Tram "Shiritsu Taiikukan-mae Station"
水前寺江津湖公園.access_car: About 15 minutes from Kyushu Expressway "Mashiki Kumamoto Airport IC"

西之久保公園.tagline: Discover the breathtaking harmony of 20,000 irises and hydrangeas in Amakusa's lush terraced oasis.
西之久保公園.desc:
Nishinokubo Park, nestled in the scenic beauty of Amakusa, is a vast agricultural landscape park ingeniously crafted from ancient natural valley terracing. Every June, the park's famous 20,000 ‘hanashōbu’ and the thousands of ‘ajisai’ planted on the surrounding slopes bloom in a miraculous synchrony.

The most unmissable visual spectacle is the ‘dual interpretation of terraced flower seas and colorful floral carpets.’ From the elevated wooden walkway, gaze down at the valley floor, where a massive carpet of purple and pure white irises meets the vibrant wall of hydrangeas cascading down the hillside, creating a stunning visual impact with depth and dimension.

As the park is developed within the valley terracing, the walking paths include wooden steps and natural dirt trails, so comfortable walking shoes are highly recommended. Seasoned photographers also suggest visiting during the rainy season when the flowers' saturation is at its peak, capturing a scene that resembles a paradise on earth.
西之久保公園.address: 熊本県天草市本渡町本戸馬場1909-1
西之久保公園.access_train: Take the express bus ‘A-Line’ from Kumamoto Station for about 2.5 hours to Amakusa Hondu.

住吉自然公園.tagline: Discover a hidden gem by the sea, where ancient shrines meet 2,000 blooming hydrangeas.
住吉自然公園.desc:
住吉自然公園 is located along the coast of Uto City, extending into the mysterious waters of Ariake Sea on a small peninsula. Every June, during the rainy season, the ancient住吉神社, perched on the peninsula, bursts into bloom with up to 2,000 hydrangeas, making it a renowned local seaside spot.

The most iconic view features the vibrant red torii gate, the historic shrine, the sea, and a tapestry of flowers all in one frame. Bright flower clusters embrace ancient stone lanterns and mossy pathways, with the endless blue of Ariake Sea as a backdrop. The flowers sway gently in the sea breeze, showcasing a pure and ethereal beauty.

The park is free to visit year-round, and the terrain around the shrine is flat and tranquil, making it easy to explore. As it is a coastal elevated area, the sea breeze can be strong, so be mindful of securing your hat or camera while taking photos. It’s best to visit on weekday mornings for the cleanest natural light for your historical snapshots.
住吉自然公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Sanriku Line 'Sumiyoshi Station'
住吉自然公園.access_car: About 30 minutes from Kyushu Expressway 'Matsubashi IC'

臼杵石佛.tagline: Discover the timeless dialogue between ancient stone Buddhas and vibrant summer blooms.
臼杵石佛.desc:
臼杵石佛 is a group of massive rock-carved Buddhas designated as a national treasure of Japan. Each early summer in June, thousands of hydrangeas bloom in harmony around the solemn stone Buddhas, painting this sacred Buddhist site with soft summer hues and exuding a deep sense of Zen tranquility.

The core visual highlight is the juxtaposition of the ancient rock-carved Buddhas with the vibrant sea of flowers. Elegant blue and purple flower clusters embrace the rustic moss-covered stones, contrasting beautifully with the millennia-old statues hidden within the rock face. By July, you can also enjoy the stunning lotus flowers blooming in the nearby pond, adding rich layers to the scenery.

As the stone Buddha site is built into the mountains, the walking paths include natural stone trails and steps. It is highly recommended to wear comfortable walking shoes when visiting to enjoy the flowers. Early morning visits on weekdays offer a chance to experience the ethereal tranquility of the mountains, with soft sunlight perfect for capturing the historical ambiance in your photos.
臼杵石佛.types: Western hydrangeas (located around the stone Buddhas and lotus pond)
臼杵石佛.address: 大分県臼杵市大字深田804-1
臼杵石佛.access_train: Take a bus for about 20 minutes from JR Usuki Station to get to "Usuki Sekibutsu."
臼杵石佛.access_car: About 5 minutes from Usuki IC on the Higashi Kyushu Expressway.

響山公園.tagline: Discover Usa's 'Hydrangea Hill' with breathtaking views of vibrant flower carpets and rolling fields.
響山公園.desc:
響山公園 is a quintessential floral oasis in Usa City, affectionately known as the local 'Hydrangea Hill' due to its abundant hydrangea plantings. Every year in June, over twenty varieties and approximately three thousand hydrangea blooms reach their peak, creating a stunning tapestry of color that spills down the hillside.

The park's most striking feature is the 'three-dimensional floral wall view' created by its elevated terrain. Stroll along the well-maintained paths to the summit, where vibrant flower clusters embrace the lush green canopy along the way. At the mountaintop observation deck, not only can you take in the breathtaking floral expanse, but you can also enjoy panoramic views of the entire Usa Plain.

The park's gentle undulations, expansive lawns, and children's play areas make it an ideal spot for a family outing in early summer. On sunny summer days, the midday sun can be intense and there is little shade, so it's highly recommended to bring a sun umbrella and stay hydrated. Visiting in the morning will provide the most comfortable walking experience.
響山公園.address: 大分県宇佐市大字四日市
響山公園.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Yanagura Station
響山公園.access_car: About 10 minutes from Usa IC on the Higashi-Kyushu Expressway

妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).tagline: Discover a hidden gem adorned with 2,000 blue blooms in a serene Buddhist sanctuary.
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).desc:
Myōgen-ji is a historic Buddhist temple nestled in the mountains of Usa City, renowned for its stunning floral displays, earning it the title of "Hydrangea Temple" in Ōita Prefecture. Each June, during the rainy season, the temple bursts into color as up to 2,000 hydrangeas bloom, transforming the sacred grounds into a vibrant spectacle.

The core aesthetic here lies in the "classical interplay of the ancient wooden hall and the blooming flowers." Vivid flower clusters stretch along both sides of the old stone path, perfectly blending with the moss, stone Jizō, and lanterns. As the temple's sutras resonate, the harmonious scene exudes a profound sense of tranquil Zen.

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making for an easy stroll that takes about forty minutes in total. During the blooming season, the temple offers special exquisite goshuin stamps. Visiting in the early morning on weekdays allows for a more intimate experience, with fewer visitors and ideal lighting for early summer photography.
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).types: 200 varieties including Western hydrangeas and mountain hydrangeas
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).fee: 大人 300 円 (僅紫陽花季期間)
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).address: 大分県臼杵市大字福良581-2
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).access_train: About 5 minutes by taxi from JR Usuki Station
妙顯寺 (紫陽花寺).access_car: About 10 minutes from the Higashi-Kyushu Expressway Usuki IC

山之口紫陽花公園.tagline: Experience a breathtaking tapestry of 28,000 vibrant hydrangeas at the summit of Miyakonojo!
山之口紫陽花公園.desc:
Yamanokuchi Ajisai Park is located in Yamanokuchi-cho, Miyakonojo City. Nestled against the expansive natural hillsides, the park is home to over 20 varieties of approximately 28,000 hydrangeas. During the peak bloom season in late June, the entire valley is blanketed in a stunning sea of colorful flowers, creating a visually striking spectacle.

The park's standout feature is the perfect blend of the "vintage castle observation tower and the three-dimensional flower sea." At the highest point of the park, there is an observation deck designed to resemble an ancient castle keep. As the vibrant flower walls undulate along the steep slopes, they frame the historic tower, creating a magnificent landscape that is a feast for the eyes.

Since the entire park is set on natural hilly terrain, the flower-viewing trail to the castle observation tower involves climbing several slopes. Be sure to wear non-slip casual shoes. The summer sun can be intense with little shade, so it's highly recommended to bring a sun umbrella and allocate about 1.5 hours to leisurely explore the area.
山之口紫陽花公園.address: 宮崎県都城市山之口町花木1630
山之口紫陽花公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Yamanokuchi Station
山之口紫陽花公園.access_car: About 5 minutes from Miyazaki Expressway Yamanokuchi Smart IC

桃源郷岬.tagline: Experience breathtaking blooms and unbeatable ocean views at this hidden gem!
桃源郷岬.desc:
桃源郷岬 is a world-class private flower viewing spot, nestled away from the hustle and bustle. Each early summer, along the rugged coastline and cliff hills overlooking Hyuga Beach, an astonishing two million hydrangeas bloom in a spectacular display, making it one of the finest in Kyushu.

The most breathtaking sight here is the perfect harmony between the unbeatable Pacific Ocean view and the exquisite flower sea. Strolling along the park's pathways, you'll be surrounded by a vibrant carpet of colorful blooms while gazing at the endless blue sea. As the sea breeze blows, the blue and purple flower wall sways gracefully, creating a magnificent scene.

During the blooming season, an entrance fee is charged. Due to the natural undulations of the coastline, the walking paths are mostly dirt trails, so it's highly recommended to wear comfortable running shoes and be prepared for mosquitoes. The sunset hours are widely regarded as the most beautiful time for photography, capturing the golden sea surface alongside the sunset-drenched, colorful flower sea.
桃源郷岬.address: 宮崎県東臼杵郡門川町遠見半島
桃源郷岬.access_train: About 15 minutes by taxi from JR 'Kadogawa Station'
桃源郷岬.access_car: About 15 minutes from 'Kadogawa IC' on the Higashi Kyushu Expressway

平和台公園.tagline: Discover the enchanting blooms of Miyazaki's green lung, where ancient clay figures meet vibrant hydrangeas.
平和台公園.desc:
Heiwadai Kōen is a comprehensive nature park renowned for its magnificent Peace Tower. Every June during the rainy season, thousands of hydrangeas quietly bloom around the expansive pond and walking paths, painting a unique early summer scene exclusive to Miyazaki.

The most distinctive visual highlight is the "mystical blend of ancient clay figures and blooming flowers." Amidst the dense shade and moss-covered stones, replicas of Haniwa (clay figurines) from Japan's Kofun period emerge, with vibrant flower clusters blooming at their feet, creating a whimsical, fairy-tale-like atmosphere.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained walking paths make it very friendly for the elderly and strollers. The hydrangea area is mostly located under comfortable shade, providing a refreshing walking experience. On rainy days, the contrast between the lush greenery and blooming flowers becomes even more vivid, making it perfect for leisurely strolls and photography on tranquil weekday mornings.
平和台公園.address: 宮崎県宮崎市下北方町越ヶ迫6146
平和台公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR Miyazaki Station for about 20 minutes to "Heiwadai."
平和台公園.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Miyazaki IC on the Miyazaki Expressway.

東郷あじさい坂.tagline: Wander the historic Togo Ajisai Slope, immersed in a colorful tunnel of classic moss-covered stone steps and blooming flowers.
東郷あじさい坂.desc:
Togo Ajisai Slope is located in the historically rich Satsumasendai City, Togo Town. This hidden gem, lovingly preserved by local residents, features thousands of hydrangeas planted along a natural, ancient gravel slope. Each June, this place transforms into a stunning floral tunnel.

The core visual charm lies in the "rustic moss-covered stone steps intertwined with a vibrant sea of flowers." The colorful blooms rise and fall with the slope, beautifully complemented by ancient stone lanterns and historical monuments along the way. After the gentle rains of the plum rain season, the flower petals, adorned with water droplets, appear especially vivid against the lush greenery.

Since the main flower viewing path is concentrated on the sloped natural stone road, it can be slippery when wet. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip casual or athletic shoes when visiting. This spot offers a relatively tranquil experience for tourists, with weekday mornings being the best time to enjoy its unique ethereal and serene atmosphere for photography.
東郷あじさい坂.address: 鹿児島県薩摩川内市東郷町斧渕
東郷あじさい坂.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Kawauchi Station" for about 20 minutes to "Togo Shisho-mae," then walk for about 10 minutes.
東郷あじさい坂.access_car: About 50 minutes from Kyushu Expressway "Yokokawa IC."

出水市紫陽花路 (あじさいロード).tagline: Drive slowly along Ajisai Road, where colorful blooms stretch for miles like a vibrant ribbon.
出水市紫陽花路 (あじさいロード).desc:
Ajisai Road in Izumi City is a scenic corridor that winds around Takao no Mizukō (Higashikawauchi Reservoir) for several kilometers, showcasing the beauty of early summer. Each June, thousands of hydrangeas bloom in unison along the roadside, transforming this tranquil green mountain path into a tapestry of vibrant colors.

The visual highlight is the "three-dimensional clusters of flowers" that envelop you while driving or strolling. The water-blue and deep purple flower walls rise and fall with the road, resembling a colorful ribbon wrapping around the serene surface of the reservoir. The lively sea of flowers contrasts beautifully with the majestic distant mountains, creating a stunning landscape with dynamic lines.

As this spot is essentially an open public road, some sections lack dedicated sidewalks. When walking to admire the flowers or find the perfect angle for photos, be sure to stay alert for passing vehicles. It's recommended to visit on weekday mornings outside of peak hours, when the sunlight is soft and ideal for photography, making it easier to capture safe and stunning moments.
出水市紫陽花路 (あじさいロード).address: 鹿児島県出水市上大川内
出水市紫陽花路 (あじさいロード).access_train: About 20 minutes by taxi from JR・Hizenkō Railway "Izumi Station"
出水市紫陽花路 (あじさいロード).access_car: About 25 minutes from the Minamikyushu Nishi-Kai Highway "Izumi IC"

吉野公園.tagline: Discover a vibrant oasis where majestic Sakurajima volcano meets a sea of blossoms.
吉野公園.desc:
Yoshino Park, perched on the Kuno Highlands, is a massive urban park recognized as one of Japan's top 100 city parks, offering stunning views of Kinko Bay. Every June, the park's expansive pathways and flower fields burst into color with around 40,000 hydrangeas in full bloom, transforming the lush landscape into a vibrant spectacle.

The park is renowned for its breathtaking view of the smoking Sakurajima volcano framed by a dreamlike sea of flowers. From the spacious central plaza, visitors can capture the grandeur of the distant Sakurajima alongside the vivid blue and purple hydrangeas, creating a scene that balances majestic beauty with the gentle charm of early summer.

With its vast, flat terrain and well-paved paths, the park is perfect for families with strollers and elderly visitors. It features a large fountain and a children's play area, making it ideal for a leisurely visit. Plan to spend about 1.5 hours here, especially on bright weekday mornings when the light is perfect for capturing the stunning volcanic flower landscape.
吉野公園.address: 鹿児島県鹿児島市吉野町3339
吉野公園.access_train: About 30 minutes from JR Kagoshima Chuo Station via Nankoku Kotsu Bus (toward Yoshino Park)
吉野公園.access_car: About 15 minutes from Satsuma Yoshida IC on the Kyushu Expressway

赤沢のあじさいロード.address: 岩手県紫波郡紫波町赤沢
赤沢のあじさいロード.access_train: 15 minutes by taxi from JR "Shiwa Chuo Station"
赤沢のあじさいロード.access_car: About 10 minutes from Tohoku Expressway "Shiwa IC"
赤沢のあじさいロード.tagline: Discover a stunning, colorful road of hydrangeas in Shiwa Town, blooming for miles in early summer.
赤沢のあじさいロード.desc:
Akazawa Ajisai Road is located in the Akazawa area of Shiwa Town, Iwate Prefecture. This picturesque country road stretches for several kilometers, lined with an abundance of hydrangeas lovingly planted by local residents. Each early summer, it transforms into a breathtaking "purple road tunnel."

The highlight is the "endless flower wall" that flanks the road. Whether you're strolling or driving slowly, vibrant hydrangea blooms are within reach on both sides. The gradient of blue and purple flowers flows along the undulating road, creating a beautiful countryside scene perfectly blended with nature.

As this spot is essentially an open public road, please be mindful of traffic while walking and taking photos to ensure your safety. It's recommended to visit during weekdays and off-peak hours for a more leisurely experience, allowing you to safely capture the expansive beauty of the blooming flowers.

松澤神社.address: 岩手県一関市千厩町千厩前田6
松澤神社.access_train: 15-minute walk from JR Ofunato Line "Chigusa Station"
松澤神社.access_car: About 45 minutes from Tohoku Expressway "Ichinoseki IC"
松澤神社.tagline: Discover a hidden sanctuary in Chigusa, where giant cedar paths meet the serene beauty of blooming flowers.
松澤神社.desc:
Matsuzawa Jinja, nestled in Chigusa, Ichinoseki City, Iwate Prefecture, is a historic shrine shrouded in mystery and tranquility. From late June to early July, the shrine's path and surrounding area come alive with hydrangeas, adding soft summer hues to this sacred space.

The most captivating aspect here is the harmonious blend of "sacred architecture and blooming flowers." Vivid or subtle clusters of hydrangeas embrace the ancient wooden shrine, the red torii gate, and stone lanterns, all under the protective shade of towering cedar trees, showcasing a serene and spiritually rich aesthetic of traditional Japanese religion.

This is a private retreat away from the hustle and bustle, with relatively few tourists, making it perfect for discerning travelers seeking quality and avoiding crowds. The shrine grounds are mostly gravel paths, so casual shoes are recommended. Please maintain silence during your visit to preserve the solemn atmosphere for the local guardian deity.

陣ヶ岡歴史公園.address: 岩手県紫波郡紫波町宮手陣ヶ岡69
陣ヶ岡歴史公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Furudate Station
陣ヶ岡歴史公園.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes from Tohoku Expressway Shiwa IC
陣ヶ岡歴史公園.tagline: Explore the historic site of Minamoto no Yoritomo's campaign, overlooking the vibrant hydrangea sea in Shiwa Town.
陣ヶ岡歴史公園.desc:
Jin'gaoka Rekishi Koen, located in Iwate Prefecture's Shiwa Town, is a historic battlefield site where Minamoto no Yoritomo established his main camp during the conquest of the Fujiwara clan. Each year, in early July, this legendary hill is blanketed by a stunning sea of hydrangeas in full bloom.

The park's core feature is the intertwining of historical monuments and summer floral beauty. Numerous historical memorials and legendary ruins are scattered throughout the park, surrounded by colorful clusters of hydrangeas. Climbing to this strategically located high ground offers breathtaking views of Shiwa Town's picturesque rural landscape.

According to official estimates, the blooming period is relatively short and concentrated in early July. Due to the park's elevated terrain, the panoramic views are exceptional, but there is limited shade during the summer. It is recommended to visit in the morning or evening for a cooler experience, as the soft sunlight beautifully highlights the majesty and charm of this plateau park.

鋸山 日本寺.tagline: A sacred cliffside sanctuary where giant Buddha sculptures meet the ethereal beauty of wild hydrangeas.
鋸山 日本寺.desc:
Nokogiriyama Nihonji is an ancient spiritual site nestled among steep cliffs, boasting a history of over 1,300 years. During the rainy season from late June to early July, around 3,000 wild hydrangeas burst into bloom, adding gentle colors to this solemn and sacred domain, unique to early summer.

The most breathtaking visual highlight here is the "epic frame of the giant stone Buddha, cliffs, and sea of flowers." Rustic stone lanterns, cliffside Buddha statues, and steep rock walls are tightly surrounded by blue and purple blooms, creating a perfect blend of historical depth and serene Zen ambiance.

As the entire temple is built against the steep slopes of Nokogiriyama, the approach is mostly made up of natural stone steps. It is highly recommended to wear professional non-slip hiking shoes. Mornings in the mountains can be foggy, and the mystical atmosphere created by the stone Buddha and blooming flowers in the mist is a favorite time for experienced photographers.
鋸山 日本寺.address: 千葉県安房郡鋸南町元名184-4
鋸山 日本寺.access_train: Approximately 45 minutes on foot from JR Uchibo Line "Yoshida Station" (recommended to take the Nokogiriyama Ropeway and then walk)
鋸山 日本寺.access_car: About 10 minutes from the Futtsu-Kanaya Road "Kyonan-Yoshida IC"

あけぼの山農業公園.tagline: Stroll through Kashiwa's enchanting European-style pond, where vintage windmills meet vibrant floral wonderlands.
あけぼの山農業公園.desc:
Akebono-yama Agricultural Park is a vast space dedicated to exquisite seasonal gardening and leisure farming. Every early summer in June, thousands of hydrangeas bloom quietly around the park's iconic European windmill and along the water's edge, transforming the lush greenery into a colorful and lively spectacle.

The park's standout feature is the "perfect blend of exotic windmills, waterfronts, and flower fields." The vibrant Western hydrangeas bloom by the water's edge, harmonizing with the blooming water lilies in the pond and the majestic Dutch windmill in the distance, creating a fairytale atmosphere that is a favorite for social media photography.

The park's flat terrain and well-planned walking paths make it very stroller and elder-friendly. To avoid the afternoon crowds and capture the clearest reflections of the windmill on the water, it is highly recommended to arrive early on weekday mornings when the light is brightest and most vibrant.
あけぼの山農業公園.count: about 1,000 plants (next to the waterwheel path)
あけぼの山農業公園.access_train: Take the JR train to Abiko Station, then transfer to Bando Bus (toward Akebono-yama Agricultural Park) and get off at the last stop.
あけぼの山農業公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Joban Expressway "Kashiwa IC."

千葉市昭和の森.tagline: Step into a vast forest twenty times the size of Tokyo Dome and discover a sea of hydrangeas.
千葉市昭和の森.desc:
Chiba City Showa no Mori is an expansive nature park, covering an area twenty times larger than Tokyo Dome. Every year, during the rainy season from mid-June to early July, the park's vast forests and the 'Hydrangea Valley' trail come alive with around 3,000 blooming hydrangeas, transforming the landscape into a refreshing floral paradise.

The park's core visual feature is the interplay of 'dappled sunlight and wildflower meadows.' Stroll along the natural wooden pathways built into the mountainside, where colorful flower clusters hide among towering ancient trees and lush moss. Sunlight filters through the high canopies, creating a stunning display of layered ecological beauty.

Due to the park's expansive nature, it's perfect for planning a full-day picnic on the lawn or a leisurely bike ride. The mountain trails are generally cool and comfortable year-round, but early summer brings more mosquitoes, so be sure to wear comfortable walking shoes and bring mosquito repellent. Weekday mornings are particularly tranquil.
千葉市昭和の森.address: 千葉県千葉市緑区土気町34
千葉市昭和の森.access_train: About a 30-minute walk from JR Sotobo Line 'Toki Station'
千葉市昭和の森.access_car: About 5 minutes from Chiba Sotobo Expressway 'Itakura IC'

地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.tagline: Experience breathtaking views where the earth meets the horizon, surrounded by vibrant hydrangeas.
地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.desc:
Perched on a high bluff in Choshi City, this spot offers a premier vantage point to witness the earth's curvature. Each early summer, the paths leading to the mountaintop observation deck are lined with blooming hydrangeas, creating a stunning floral corridor that dances in the salty sea breeze.

The most striking sight is the "360-degree dialogue between the ocean view and the sea of flowers." Stroll slowly down the gentle slope, where colorful flower walls perfectly frame the old lighthouse and the deep blue Pacific Ocean, creating a harmonious blend of nature's beauty. This unique island landscape showcases an expansive sense of space, combining softness and strength.

Due to its coastal location, the sea breeze can be quite strong year-round, so be sure to secure your hat while taking photos. Visiting at dusk is particularly romantic, as the sunset casts a golden-red hue over the ocean and the vibrant flower sea, making it a rare hidden gem for enjoying coastal flower views in Japan.
地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.hours: 09:00～18:30 (展望館時間，廣場區終日開放)
地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.fee: 免費 (展望館大人 420 円)
地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.access_train: 15-minute walk from Inubō Station on the Choshi Electric Railway
地球の丸く見える丘ふれあい広場.access_car: About 1 hour from Sawara Katori IC on the Higashi Kanto Expressway

神代植物公園.tagline: Stroll through Musashino's botanical treasure, discovering the exquisite artistry of the 'Hydrangea Garden.'
神代植物公園.desc:
神代植物公園 is one of Tokyo's premier botanical gardens, preserving the rich natural landscape of Musashino. Within its grounds, the 'Hydrangea Garden' carefully cultivates a diverse array of hydrangeas, from Western varieties to native Japanese mountain hydrangeas, transforming into a vibrant sea of colors during the early summer rainy season.

The park's charm lies in its harmonious integration with nature. As you wander along the comfortable shaded paths, delicate flower clusters bloom amidst lush trees and moss, showcasing the garden's unique sophistication and elegant horticultural aesthetics under the dappled sunlight filtering through the canopy.

The hydrangea area is pleasantly cool, even on sunny early summer days, thanks to the abundance of tall trees providing shade. Given the park's vast expanse, it's recommended to set aside half a day to explore the greenhouses and the grand rose garden. Visiting on weekday mornings offers the best opportunity for quiet and leisurely botanical photography.
神代植物公園.types: Western hydrangeas, mountain hydrangeas, wild species
神代植物公園.address: 東京都調布市深大寺元町2-17-4
神代植物公園.access_train: Take the Keio Line to Chofu Station, then transfer to a bus (towards Kichijoji Station) and get off at 'Jindai Shokubutsu Koen Mae.'
神代植物公園.access_car: About 5 minutes from Chofu IC on the Chuo Expressway.

國營昭和紀念公園.tagline: Stroll along the Hydrangea Path, where 9,000 blooms enchant beside wooden bridges and lush forests.
國營昭和紀念公園.desc:
Showa Kinen Park spans an impressive 39 times the size of Tokyo Dome. Located north of the "Koryu Plaza," the "Hydrangea Path" is the centerpiece of early summer, featuring around 60 varieties and up to 9,000 hydrangeas, creating a vibrant tapestry of colors during the rainy season.

The core visual is the perfect harmony of "classic wooden bridges, streams, and a sea of flowers." Blue, white, and pink flower clusters bloom along the gentle waterfront and winding paths, intertwining with the lush green canopies above, making you feel as if you've stepped into a refreshing and exquisite early summer landscape painting, uplifting your spirits.

Given the park's vast area, it's highly recommended to rent a bicycle at the entrance or take the park's mini train for easy navigation. The shaded surroundings of the Hydrangea Path provide a comfortable escape from the summer heat, making it an ideal walking route for leisurely travelers to enjoy in both sunny and rainy weather, especially in the morning.
國營昭和紀念公園.access_train: About a 2-minute walk from JR Ome Line "Nishi Tachikawa Station" (Nishi Tachikawa Exit)
國營昭和紀念公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from Chuo Expressway "Kunitachi Fuchu IC"

伊豆大島 アジサイレインボーライン.tagline: A four-kilometer floral wonder on a volcanic island, showcasing the deep blue hydrangeas born from unique soil.
伊豆大島 アジサイレインボーライン.desc:
Located on the Tokyo island of Izu Oshima, the Ajisai Rainbow Line is a scenic road stretching four kilometers towards the Mikinokawa Skyline. Flanking the road are countless native "shima ajisai" (island hydrangeas) that bloom in unison every year from mid-June to early July, transforming the area into a true "ocean rainbow flower path."

Thanks to the unique basalt soil of this volcanic island, the hydrangeas here display an exceptionally rich and vibrant deep blue hue. On the often foggy mountain roads, the deep blue flower clusters emerge mysteriously from the mist, creating an unparalleled and mystical landscape unique to Japan.

Due to the higher elevation, the blooming season here is typically slightly later than in central Tokyo. Renting a car or biking is the best way to enjoy the flowers, but be cautious of the winding mountain roads. Even on foggy days, don't be disheartened; the blue island hydrangeas take on an even more enchanting and romantic quality in the mist.
伊豆大島 アジサイレインボーライン.address: 東京都大島町泉津木積場
伊豆大島 アジサイレインボーライン.access_train: Take a high-speed jet ferry to "Oshima Motomachi Port" or "Okada Port," then take a bus or rent a car.
伊豆大島 アジサイレインボーライン.access_car: About a 20-minute drive from Motomachi Port.

井の頭恩賜公園.tagline: Experience the elegance of early summer blooms along the waterfront in this urban oasis of Kichijoji.
井の頭恩賜公園.desc:
Inokashira Onshi Park is one of Japan's top urban parks, serving as the green lung right next to the bustling Kichijoji. Every June, wild Western hydrangeas quietly bloom around the starting point of the Kanda River and along the waterfront paths, painting the urban oasis in gentle shades of blue and purple.

The unique joy of flower viewing here lies in "admiring the flowers from the water's surface." Visitors can rent swan boats or rowboats to glide across the lake, enjoying the vibrant flower clusters cascading down the shore, with reflections of lush greenery and shimmering blossoms creating an exquisitely elegant early summer riverside scene.

On weekends, the park attracts large crowds of nature lovers, so for a high-quality boating and flower-viewing experience, it’s highly recommended to visit on a weekday morning. The area is dotted with stylish cafes, making it perfect to grab a cup of rich coffee and leisurely stroll along the Kanda River's waterfront—a truly authentic Tokyo half-day adventure.
井の頭恩賜公園.address: 東京都武蔵野市御殿山1-18-31
井の頭恩賜公園.access_train: About a 5-minute walk from JR and Keio Kichijoji Station

町田藥師池公園.tagline: Discover a timeless Tokyo gem where history and nature bloom in harmony.
町田藥師池公園.desc:
Recognized as one of the "New Tokyo 100 Views," Yakushiike Park is a renowned garden that beautifully merges historical landscapes from the Edo period with nature. The park features a "Hydrangea Garden" surrounded by approximately 2,200 hydrangeas, showcasing a rustic aesthetic during the rainy season.

The visual highlight is the "classical dialogue between the nationally designated important cultural property, the old Nagai family house, and the blooming flowers." The thatched-roof of the ancient Nagai residence, the vermillion Taiko Bridge, and the clear Yakushi Pond are enveloped by blue and purple flower clusters, transporting visitors back in time to Japan's traditional landscapes.

The park's terrain naturally undulates with the valley, featuring stone steps and slopes along the walking paths. It's highly recommended to wear comfortable walking shoes. In early summer, the "Oga Lotus" and iris flowers also bloom in succession, so plan for about 1.5 hours to stroll, especially in the morning when the air is rich with the serene essence of this ancient garden.
町田藥師池公園.access_train: Take the Odakyu Line to Machida Station, then transfer to a bus (toward Honmachida) and get off at "Yakushiike."
町田藥師池公園.access_car: About 20 minutes from the Tomei Expressway "Yokohama Machida IC."

都立向島百花園.tagline: Discover the elegant garden cherished by Edo's literary figures, where the beauty of mountain hydrangeas unfolds gracefully.
都立向島百花園.desc:
Mukojima Hyakkaen is an ancient garden established during the Edo period, rich in literary charm. Unlike typical grand attractions, this serene spot features classical bamboo fences and pathways, delicately planted with various native Japanese mountain hydrangeas and lacecap hydrangeas, showcasing an elegant classical plant aesthetic.

The essence of this garden lies in its "unconventional literary scenery." The simple yet resilient mountain hydrangeas perfectly complement the lush pine and bamboo greenery, along with the classical wooden architecture of the Onarizukuri. In the gentle rain of the rainy season, the petals sway among the moss, beautifully embodying the Edo period's aesthetic of "iki" and tranquility.

The garden's compact layout and flat terrain make it easy for families with elders or children to stroll without burden. The tranquil atmosphere, distinct from the bustling city, allows for deeper, story-filled captures of traditional Japanese scenery, especially when walking slowly under a parasol along the bamboo fence on rainy days.
都立向島百花園.types: over 20 varieties including Western hydrangeas and mountain hydrangeas
都立向島百花園.access_train: About an 8-minute walk from Tobu Skytree Line "Higashi Mukojima Station"

洗足池公園.tagline: Stroll along the historical waterside of Senzokuike, enchanted by the gentle breeze and vibrant hydrangeas.
洗足池公園.desc:
Senzokuike Park is built around the famous natural spring, Senzokuike, located in Ota Ward. This serene spot was also the beloved retreat and final resting place of the renowned Bakumatsu statesman, Katsu Kaishu. Every June, the colorful hydrangeas bloom along the eastern promenade by the pond and around Katsu Kaishu's grave, adorning the clear waters with their stunning beauty.

The park's highlight is the "panoramic waterside view intertwined with blooming flowers." Strolling along the well-maintained wooden walkway by the lake, you can enjoy the refreshing breeze while gazing at the iconic Akatori gate, boats on the lake, and the colorful blooms reflected in the water. The harmony of movement and stillness creates a captivating spatial experience.

The lakeside walking path is fully paved, making it accessible for strollers and wheelchairs. On sunny afternoons, you can enjoy boating or capture the flower fields from the center of the lake; during dusk, the sunset casts a golden-red hue over the lake and hydrangeas, creating a magical moment known only to the locals.
洗足池公園.access_train: A 2-minute walk from Senzokuike Station on the Tokyu Ikegami Line

箱根玻璃之森美術館.tagline: Where nature meets Venetian craftsmanship, bask in the eternal sparkle of crystal hydrangeas.
箱根玻璃之森美術館.desc:
Nestled in Hakone Sengokuhara, this is Japan's first European-style museum dedicated to Venetian glass art. Every early summer, the museum astounds visitors by beautifully blending 'real hydrangeas' with exquisite 'crystal glass hydrangeas' crafted by top artisans in its lush gardens.

The highlight is the 'prismatic dreamlight refracted by sunlight.' Composed of thousands of crystal glass particles, the glass hydrangea balls sway gently in the breeze of the Hakone plateau, reflecting dazzling brilliance like diamonds in natural light, intertwined with vibrant real flowers for a truly romantic experience.

A unique feature of the crystal glass hydrangeas is their resilience to weather; even on rainy days during the monsoon season, they radiate pure light. The garden's outdoor café regularly features live performances of Italian folk music, making it the perfect spot to sip afternoon tea while gazing at the shimmering sea of crystal flowers, offering an ultimate indulgence.
箱根玻璃之森美術館.types: Crystal hydrangeas (glass products) and Western hydrangeas
箱根玻璃之森美術館.address: 神奈川県足柄下郡箱根町仙石原940-48
箱根玻璃之森美術館.access_train: Take a bus from JR Odawara Station for about 40 minutes to 'Hakone Glass no Mori.'
箱根玻璃之森美術館.access_car: About 20 minutes from Tomei Expressway 'Gotemba IC.'

三溪園.tagline: Step into a national treasure garden and capture the timeless beauty of the ancient three-story pagoda with wild hydrangeas.
三溪園.desc:
Created by entrepreneur Hara Sankei, this expansive garden is a source of pride for Yokohama, designated as a national scenic spot. Every early June, hundreds of wild and mountain hydrangeas bloom peacefully around historical buildings and traditional houses relocated from Kyoto and Kamakura, exuding a deep Eastern aesthetic.

The most iconic view here is undoubtedly the classic framing of the 'Old Tomyoji Three-Story Pagoda surrounded by flowers.' Vibrant blooms cluster around ancient moss, wooden structures, and the expansive pond. During the rainy season, the entire garden is enveloped in a mist, resembling a beautiful ink wash painting with profound meaning.

The garden is vast and rich in historical relics, so it's recommended to set aside at least 1.5 hours for a leisurely visit. In early summer, the 'water lilies' in the pond also reach their blooming peak, making it highly advisable to visit on a weekday morning to fully enjoy the serene atmosphere unique to this ancient garden.
三溪園.address: 神奈川県横浜市中区本牧三之谷58-1
三溪園.access_train: Take a JR train to Negishi Station, then transfer to a bus for about 10 minutes to Honmoku, followed by a 10-minute walk.
三溪園.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes from the Honmoku-futo IC on the Shuto Expressway.

橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).tagline: Discover a breathtaking realm of 300 flower varieties, where late-blooming roses and hydrangeas create a stunning floral symphony.
橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).desc:
Yokohama English Garden is a European-style botanical garden renowned for its sophisticated horticultural aesthetics in the Kanto region. The garden boasts around 300 varieties and approximately 3,000 hydrangeas, including rare antique colors and exquisite double-petal types, creating a vibrant sea of flowers in early summer.

The most breathtaking sight here is the "gorgeous collaboration of late-blooming roses and early summer flowers." In early June, the famous English rose archway is adorned with a colorful wall of hydrangeas. The lingering fragrance of roses harmonizes with the vivid blooms, transporting visitors to a secret garden of a noble castle.

Every corner of the garden is meticulously designed, with a layered color palette perfect for Instagram-worthy photos and stylish outfits. Since the overlapping bloom period in early June is the peak of the year, it's highly recommended to visit on a weekday right when the garden opens for the best chance at capturing pristine scenes.
橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).types: about 300 varieties including Western,額, and mountain hydrangeas
橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).address: 神奈川県横浜市西区西平沼町6-1
橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Hiranumabashi Station on the Sotetsu Line
橫濱英式花園 (横浜イングリッシュガーデン).access_car: Dedicated parking available (note that spaces are limited during peak times)

神奈川縣立大船植物園.tagline: Stroll through a refined botanical paradise near Kamakura, exploring the exquisite world of hydrangeas.
神奈川縣立大船植物園.desc:
This historic floral center was once a agricultural research facility from the Taisho era. The "Ajisai-en" within boasts Japan's finest plant collection, featuring everything from native wild species to the stunning Western varieties cultivated in Europe and America. During the rainy season in June, it transforms into a vibrant sea of colors.

The highlight here is the garden's unique "systematic and refined arrangement." Each hydrangea is accompanied by a clear name tag, and the clusters are neatly organized by shades of white, blue, and purple, resembling a gradient watercolor masterpiece. It's a paradise for flower gardening enthusiasts and travelers who want to closely compare the differences in varieties.

The park's walking paths are fully paved and accessible, making it perfect for families with elders or strollers to enjoy a leisurely stroll. With ample grassy areas and shaded seating, you can relax at the on-site café after a morning of flower viewing, ensuring a comfortable experience.
神奈川縣立大船植物園.types: Western, Ougon, Yamajidori, and Tamajidori hydrangeas

小田原花卉花園.tagline: Stroll through a tropical paradise and be amazed by the 200-meter 'Ume Hayazaki Path' of vibrant greens and blues.
小田原花卉花園.desc:
This comprehensive botanical park is marked by its massive glass-domed tropical greenhouse. The most unique early summer attraction here is the 200-meter-long 'Ume Ajisai Path' (梅林紫陽花小道). Every June, around 180 varieties and thousands of hydrangeas bloom in harmony beneath the lush ume trees.

The visual highlight is the 'Blue Ocean' beneath the verdant ume grove. The park has intentionally chosen deep blue and aqua-colored Western hydrangeas, creating a stunning sea of blue flowers that stretches out under the gentle shade of the ume trees, providing a refreshing escape from the muggy rainy season.

Entry to the park is free, and there is a large free parking lot available. In addition to the outdoor blue hydrangea path, the tropical rainforest greenhouse, open year-round (separate admission required), is also a must-see. It’s perfect for a visit on rainy days, offering the dual enjoyment of indoor shelter and outdoor flower viewing in the rain.
小田原花卉花園.address: 神奈川県小田原市久野3798-5
小田原花卉花園.access_train: 20-minute walk from Izu Hakone Railway 'Iidaoka Station'

市立相模原麻溝公園.tagline: Escape the ordinary and soar above a sea of 200 flower varieties in a breathtaking floral forest stroll.
市立相模原麻溝公園.desc:
Sagamihara Asamizoko Park is a vast oasis of greenery. The park boasts around 8,000 hydrangeas across 200 different varieties. From late May to mid-June, the park's other signature flower, the clematis, blooms, creating a vibrant tapestry of early summer blossoms.

The park's most iconic experience is ascending the landmark "Sagamihara Green Tower" observation deck. Visitors can enjoy a 360-degree aerial view of the colorful hydrangea garden below. On the ground, you can stroll through flower pathways taller than yourself, surrounded by stunning floral walls.

Entry to the park is free, and it features a child-friendly mini-animal interaction area and large adventure playgrounds, making it perfect for family outings on weekends. Due to the park's expansive grounds, wearing comfortable walking shoes is highly recommended, especially for the best light for aerial photography in the morning.
市立相模原麻溝公園.types: Western, macrophylla, mountain hydrangeas, and Annabelle, among others
市立相模原麻溝公園.address: 神奈川県相模原市南区麻溝台2317-1
市立相模原麻溝公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR "Haratama Station"
市立相模原麻溝公園.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Sagamihara Aikawa IC on the Chuo Expressway

報國寺.tagline: Step into the enchanting realm of the 'Bamboo Temple' and be captivated by the serene beauty of lush green bamboo and solitary blue hydrangeas.
報國寺.desc:
Hōkoku-ji is a world-renowned ancient temple of the Rinzai sect, famous for its stunning grove of over two thousand towering Moso bamboo, earning it the nickname 'Bamboo Temple.' Each June, the ancient courtyard and mossy stone paths come alive with blooming hydrangeas, adding a cool summer hue to this sacred space.

The most breathtaking visual experience lies in the 'minimalist contrast between the dark green bamboo forest and the solitary blue flowers.' The soaring black bamboo stretches high above, while clusters of native blue hydrangeas quietly bloom amidst the moss, illuminated by dappled sunlight. The scene resembles a meticulously arranged Zen ink painting, exuding an air of serene coolness.

The famous bamboo grove tea house, Kyōgō-an, offers a chance to enjoy matcha while admiring the bamboo. Due to the influx of visitors during the flower season, it's highly recommended to arrive early and queue before the temple opens at 9 AM. Please maintain absolute silence while visiting, and those with cameras are encouraged to use negative space in their compositions to capture the ethereal Zen essence.
報國寺.address: 神奈川県鎌倉市浄明寺2-7-4
報國寺.access_train: Take the JR train to Kamakura Station, then transfer to a bus and get off at 'Jōmyō-ji,' a 3-minute walk away.
報國寺.access_car: Public transportation is recommended due to heavy traffic in Kamakura.

圓覺寺.tagline: Discover the harmony of history and nature at Kamakura's premier Zen temple.
圓覺寺.desc:
Enkōji, located just a minute's walk from Kitakamakura Station, is the esteemed second temple of the Kamakura Five Mountains. Nestled in the expansive mountains, it comes alive during the rainy season in late June to mid-July, when wild mountain hydrangeas and Edo varieties bloom in abundance, beautifully complementing the solemn, unadorned wooden halls of worship.

The core visual allure here lies in the dialogue between ancient monuments and the passage of time in nature. Whether beneath the majestic wooden Sanmon gate, beside the ancient moss-covered stone steps, or around the nationally designated treasure, the Shari-den, delicate blue flower clusters sway gently in the breeze, perfectly embodying the historical depth and tranquility unique to the ancient capital of Kamakura.

As the Great Buddha Hall and various other structures are scattered throughout the deep valley, visitors will encounter numerous natural stone steps along the way. It is highly recommended to wear good hiking shoes for better grip. A complete visit to this ancient temple takes about 1.5 hours, and the air is most ethereal early on weekdays, around 8 AM, making it the perfect time for capturing serene landscapes.
圓覺寺.address: 神奈川県鎌倉市山ノ内409
圓覺寺.access_train: About a 1-minute walk from JR 'Kitakamakura Station'

七沢森林公園.tagline: Explore Japan's largest forest at the foot of Higashi-Danzawa, where giant wild hydrangeas bloom by a stunning brick bridge.
七沢森林公園.desc:
Shichizawa Shinrin Koen is an expansive prefectural nature park, 14 times the size of Tokyo Dome, that beautifully preserves the original ecosystem of Satoyama. Every June, thousands of wild mountain hydrangeas bloom along the lengthy mountain hiking trails, creating a colorful ribbon through the lush forest.

The most breathtaking sight is the iconic "Momiji no Hashi," a giant brick suspension bridge. Standing on this magnificent vintage bridge, visitors can gaze out over the verdant tree canopy that stretches into the deep valley, with delicate wildflowers dotting the bridge and forest, creating a stunning contrast of softness and grandeur.

This is a hiking spot that requires a bit of stamina, with primitive mountain paths and varying elevations along the way. Be sure to wear professional hiking shoes or sturdy athletic shoes when coming to enjoy the flowers. The mountainous area is rich in natural ecology, and during the summer, mosquitoes can be plentiful, so it's advisable to carry insect repellent and stay hydrated.
七沢森林公園.address: 神奈川県厚木市上古沢950-3
七沢森林公園.access_train: Take the Odakyu Line to Hon-Atsugi Station, then transfer to a bus for about 35 minutes to "Shichizawa Onsen."
七沢森林公園.access_car: About 15 minutes from the Isehara-Oyama IC on the Shin-Tomei Expressway.

妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).tagline: Discover a hidden gem of hydrangeas, with 2,800 blooms surrounding the sacred Yakushi Nyorai.
妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).desc:
Nestled in the hills of Nagao, Kawasaki, Myourakuji is a renowned Tendai temple and an irreplaceable "Hydrangea Temple" for the locals. Every June, the slope leading to the main hall and various spots within the temple burst into bloom with around 2,800 hydrangeas of 28 different varieties.

The core visual experience here is the striking contrast of the "sacred stone Buddha enveloped in a sea of colorful flowers." The dense clusters of Western and mountain hydrangeas surround the ancient stone statue of Yakushi Nyorai, stone lanterns, and the wooden main hall. Amidst the blooming flowers, the solemn statue stands as if in a Buddhist paradise of the present world.

Myourakuji is tucked away in a tranquil residential area, offering a peaceful retreat compared to the bustling Kamakura city. It's perfect for travelers seeking a high-quality flower viewing experience. During the blooming season, a local hydrangea festival is held on weekends. It's recommended to visit on a rainy day during the plum rain season, as the misty atmosphere enhances the temple's mysterious charm.
妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).types: 28 varieties including Western, mountain, and lacecap hydrangeas
妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).address: 神奈川県川崎市多摩区長尾3-9-3
妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Shukugawara Station
妙樂寺 (長尾山 あじさい寺).access_car: Roads are narrow; public transportation is recommended.

十日町紫陽花公園.tagline: A vibrant tapestry of 7,000 hydrangeas, nestled in a breathtaking summer sanctuary surrounded by mountains.
十日町紫陽花公園.desc:
This oasis is a labor of love, cultivated by local residents to revitalize their village. Spanning 2.5 hectares of natural hillside, it boasts around 7,000 colorful hydrangeas that burst into bloom each early summer.

The park's visual allure lies in the stunning contrast between the majestic mountains and the vibrant sea of flowers. Stroll along the well-maintained paths, where you can be enveloped by the colorful floral walls while taking in the rich rural scenery of Tokamachi. The layers of beauty are truly captivating.

As the main flower area is located along steep natural slopes, please be mindful of your footing while enjoying the blooms. It is highly recommended to wear non-slip athletic shoes. During the festival in July, retro paper lanterns adorn the area, and visiting on weekday mornings offers the best light and refreshing atmosphere.
十日町紫陽花公園.access_train: About 15 minutes by taxi from JR Tokamachi Station
十日町紫陽花公園.access_car: About 25 minutes from the Kanetsu Expressway, Muika-machi IC

水道山・觀音山公園.tagline: Discover breathtaking views and serene hydrangea trails in the heart of nature.
水道山・觀音山公園.desc:
Suidozan-Kannonzan Park, located near Sanjo City, is a beloved green oasis that beautifully blends rich history with nature. During the rainy season, the park's winding forest paths come alive with elegant blue and purple hydrangeas, adding a refreshing touch to early summer.

The visual highlight is the "panoramic view of Takagashi Street interwoven with a tapestry of blooming flowers." From the lookout point at the summit, visitors can capture the sway of colorful flower clusters while enjoying a bird's-eye view of Sanjo City's bustling streets and the distant rolling mountains, creating a spacious and layered perspective.

Built into the mountainside, the park features gently undulating walking paths, making it perfect for a leisurely early summer hike. Towering ancient trees provide ample shade, and to avoid the afternoon crowds, it's highly recommended to visit on a weekday morning, where you can enjoy the sounds of chirping birds while leisurely capturing the most enchanting flower scenes.
水道山・觀音山公園.access_train: Take a bus from JR "Mitsuke Station" for about 10 minutes, then walk 10 minutes to "Honmachi."
水道山・觀音山公園.access_car: About 15 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway "Nakanoshima Mitsuke IC."

錢淵公園.tagline: Experience a classic Japanese garden where vibrant hydrangeas meet serene reflections.
錢淵公園.desc:
Senbutsu Park, nestled at the foot of the majestic Sakado Mountain, is a harmonious blend of rich history and nature's beauty. From late June to early July, the park's expansive pond comes alive with blooming hydrangeas, painting the entire Japanese garden in a kaleidoscope of colors.

The highlight is the enchanting interplay of the "pure Japanese waterfront, vermilion taiko bridge, and a sea of flowers." The rustic wooden tea house and elegant taiko bridge reflect on the tranquil water, beautifully complemented by the blue and pink blooms along the shore, transporting visitors into a poetic, nostalgic landscape.

The park features flat terrain and well-paved walking paths, making it ideal for strollers and seniors. During early summer, the water lilies also bloom, so set aside an hour for a leisurely stroll. The petals are most vibrant and alive in the cool morning hours, making it the perfect time for photography.
錢淵公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR 'Mukaitcho Station'
錢淵公園.access_car: Approximately 10 minutes from Kanetsu Expressway 'Mukaitcho IC'

山田池公園.tagline: Discover a historical oasis bursting with vibrant irises and thousands of hydrangeas.
山田池公園.desc:
Yamadaike Park is one of Japan's top urban parks, sprawling around the ancient Yamada Pond. Every June, the aquatic plant garden and flower fields reach their peak, showcasing around 2,000 hydrangeas in up to 50 varieties, painting the waterfront in a riot of color.

The park's main feature is the harmonious interplay of the pond's shoreline, irises, and colorful flower walls. Vibrant blooms line the wooden pathways along the water, perfectly complementing the blooming sea of purple irises nearby. When the calm water reflects the colorful flowers, the scene feels expansive and refreshingly cool.

The park's flat terrain and well-maintained trails make it ideal for families looking for a leisurely early summer outing or picnic. Since the main flower viewing paths are relatively shaded, it's highly recommended to bring an umbrella to shield against the bright summer sun. Visiting in the morning offers the best chance to capture the tranquil water surface and perfect reflections of the flower fields.
山田池公園.address: 大阪府枚方市山田池公園1-1
山田池公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Gakken Toshi Line 'Fujisaka Station'
山田池公園.access_car: About a 5-minute drive from Route 1 'Deai Intersection'

圓通寺.tagline: Discover the ethereal beauty of wild hydrangeas in the ancient temple of Enkō-ji, a hidden gem in Tanba City.
圓通寺.desc:
Enkō-ji, located in Tanba City, is a thousand-year-old temple renowned for its vibrant autumn foliage, linked to the famous shogun Ashikaga Yoshimitsu. While it is celebrated for its stunning fall colors, during the early summer rainy season in mid-June, thousands of wild hydrangeas quietly bloom along the long approach, painting the ancient temple in a unique summer hue.

The core feature here is the "religious aesthetics of towering ancient trees, Jizō stone carvings, and a sea of flowers." The elegant blue and purple flower clusters peek through the dense greenery and moss-covered rocks. When the temple's chanting resonates, the water-laden petals appear particularly vibrant against the backdrop of the ancient surroundings, creating a profound atmosphere.

The path from the main gate to the main hall is a natural gravel slope, so it is highly recommended to wear shoes with good traction when visiting. This place is far from the urban hustle and bustle, with relatively few tourists. Early morning visits on weekdays allow you to fully enjoy the unique mountain mist, making it the perfect time for cultural and ecological photography.
圓通寺.address: 兵庫県丹波市氷上町御油983
圓通寺.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR "Ishiyama Station"
圓通寺.access_car: About 10 minutes from the Hokuriku Expressway "Hiyama IC"

夙川河川敷綠地.tagline: Stroll along Nishinomiya's premier riverside, enchanted by the symphony of clear streams and vibrant floral walls this summer.
夙川河川敷綠地.desc:
Shukugawa Kasenshiki Ryokuchi stretches for several kilometers along the banks of the Shukugawa River, a renowned riverside oasis featured in Japan's top cherry blossom spots. Every early summer in June, the originally lush green pathways are transformed by thousands of blooming hydrangeas, creating a romantic floral corridor in Nishinomiya.

The visual highlight not to be missed is the interplay of "clear streams and clusters of flowers." Colorful blue and pink flower balls sway gently in the breeze by the riverside, complementing the scattered stone bridges and the crystal-clear river water. As the breeze caresses the water's surface, the scene becomes rich in layers, exuding a refreshing and elegant early summer riverside charm.

The riverside promenade is well-maintained and flat, making it very friendly for strollers and joggers, and it is open free of charge all day. With ample shade nearby, it serves as a delightful escape from the summer heat. It is highly recommended to visit in the early morning or at dusk, when the light is softest, perfect for capturing stunning reflections.
夙川河川敷綠地.count: Distributed along both banks of the river
夙川河川敷綠地.access_train: A short walk from Hankyu Shukugawa Station or JR Sakurashukugawa Station
夙川河川敷綠地.access_car: Public transport is recommended due to limited parking nearby.

御房觀音 (おふさ観音).tagline: Discover the enchanting 'Temple of Floral Mandalas' in Kashihara, where lanterns and vibrant blooms create a magical experience.
御房觀音 (おふさ観音).desc:
Gofusakannon (officially known as Kannonji) is located in Kashihara and is affectionately called the 'Temple of Floral Mandalas' for its year-round blooming flowers. Every June during the rainy season, over 3,000 rare hydrangeas burst into bloom, weaving a stunning tapestry alongside the famous spring roses, marking the most vibrant chapter of early summer.

The highlight that captivates everyone online is the limited-time 'Hydrangea and Lantern Festival.' The temple hangs over a thousand vintage lanterns above the lush flower beds, creating a scene where colorful blooms embrace the ancient wooden main hall, stone lanterns, and decorative lights, blending history with a sense of mystical fantasy—truly romantic.

The temple grounds are free to enter, with a compact and flat area that takes about forty minutes to stroll through. Starting in July, the garden seamlessly transitions into the famous Wind Chime Festival. If you want to capture both the early summer floral beauty and the exquisite limited-edition goshuin, it’s highly recommended to arrive early on a weekday morning for the best tranquil photo opportunities.
御房觀音 (おふさ観音).access_train: Take the Kintetsu train to Yamato-Yagi Station, then transfer to a bus and get off at 'Kobō,' a 5-minute walk away.
御房觀音 (おふさ観音).access_car: About 15 minutes from Kashihara Kita IC on the Keina Highway.

多摩川台公園.tagline: Discover ancient burial mounds and be dazzled by 3,000 blooming hydrangeas along the majestic Tamagawa River.
多摩川台公園.desc:
Tamagawadai Park is a historical natural park built along the Tamagawa Hills, featuring a large cluster of ancient burial mounds. The park's "Hydrangea Garden" is a premier early summer flower viewing spot along the Tokyu line, where around 3,000 hydrangeas bloom vibrantly along the hillside, creating a stunning sea of flowers.

The visual highlight is the "three-dimensional flower carpet cascading down the slope." Following the zigzagging walking path, the lush, colorful flower walls on either side rise almost to eye level, making your stroll feel like wandering through a tunnel of blossoms. At the summit's observation deck, you can take in the majestic flow of the Tamagawa River alongside the breathtaking flower sea.

Due to the significant elevation changes between the burial mounds and the observation deck, it's highly recommended to wear comfortable, grippy sports shoes. Towering ancient trees provide excellent natural shade, making early summer hikes here refreshingly cool, especially when the morning sunlight filters through the treetops, casting enchanting shadows over the flower sea.
多摩川台公園.address: 東京都大田区田園調布1-63-1
多摩川台公園.access_train: About a 1-minute walk from Tamagawa Station on the Tokyu Toyoko and Meguro Lines

花園あじさい園.tagline: Discover a breathtaking floral paradise at the foot of Mount Koya, where summer blooms unveil a stunning tapestry of hydrangeas.
花園あじさい園.desc:
Hanazono Ajisai-en is nestled in the high-altitude mountains of Katsuragi, right next to the sacred Buddhist site of Koyasan. Due to its elevation of several hundred meters and cooler climate, the blooming season here is later than in lower areas, reaching its peak in early July with a magnificent sea of 3,500 hydrangea plants and 100,000 flowers.

The main visual highlight is the "intertwining of towering cedar forests and a valley of flowers." Under the expansive cover of ancient cedar trees, pure white and blue-purple blooms spread lavishly along the winding mountain paths. When the afternoon mist rolls in, the flower sea emerges through the fog, showcasing an ethereal beauty.

This location is perfect for a special summer getaway to Koyasan, combining hot spring relaxation with flower viewing. As the entire area consists of natural hilly terrain, be prepared to climb natural slopes while enjoying the flowers, so wearing good hiking shoes is essential. Visiting at dusk offers a refreshing atmosphere, with the sunset creating a truly magical floral scene.
花園あじさい園.address: 和歌山県伊都郡かつらぎ町花園久木364-26
花園あじさい園.access_train: About 60 minutes by taxi or reservation-required bus from JR Kasada Station
花園あじさい園.access_car: About 60 minutes from the Kinki Expressway Kihoku Katsuragi IC

恋し野の里あじさい園.tagline: Immerse yourself in a vibrant tapestry of 5,000 blooms at Hashimoto's enchanting Ajisai Garden.
恋し野の里あじさい園.desc:
Koi Shino no Sato Ajisai-en is nestled in the nature-rich and myth-infused Koi region of Hashimoto City. This agricultural park is a labor of love, cultivated over the years by local residents who have come together to nurture this beautiful space, featuring an impressive 5,000 hydrangeas, making it a quintessential summer destination in Wakayama.

The park's standout feature is its "colorful three-dimensional flower bands that stretch along ancient irrigation channels and ponds." As you stroll through, the walls of blue and pink flowers undulate with the gentle slopes of the natural hills, harmonizing with the tranquil pond waters and the lush green plains in the distance, creating a picturesque Japanese summer landscape.

The park's well-maintained pathways and gentle terrain make it perfect for families to enjoy leisurely walks and picnics on weekends. During the designated weekends in late June, the lively "Koi Hydrangea Festival" and traditional live events take place. On sunny summer days, it's highly recommended to bring an umbrella for shade and visit in the morning.
恋し野の里あじさい園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Sumida Station
恋し野の里あじさい園.access_car: About a 15-minute drive from Hashimoto IC on the Keina Expressway

觀音寺 (佐伯區).tagline: Discover the historic 'Hydrangea Temple' of Itsukaichi, immersed in a vibrant sea of 300 varieties of flowers.
觀音寺 (佐伯區).desc:
觀音寺, located in Saeki-ku, Hiroshima, is an ancient temple with a rich history spanning over 1,200 years. Known locally as the 'Hydrangea Temple,' it boasts an impressive collection of hydrangeas. Every June, around 10,000 hydrangeas of up to 350 varieties burst into bloom.

The most striking visual appeal lies in the 'encyclopedic tapestry of colorful flowers.' From delicate native mountain hydrangeas to rare cultivated varieties, the blooms embrace the ancient wooden main hall, stone lanterns, and Jizo stone sculptures, weaving a tapestry of tranquility that reflects deep Eastern historical Zen.

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making it perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning stroll. The overall visit takes about 40 minutes. During peak bloom, the temple offers exquisite themed omamori (amulets), and weekday mornings are ideal for capturing stunning cultural scenes with fewer visitors.
觀音寺 (佐伯區).types: about 350 varieties of Western and mountain hydrangeas
觀音寺 (佐伯區).address: 広島県広島市佐伯区坪井町736
觀音寺 (佐伯區).access_train: 10 minutes by taxi from JR 'Itsukaichi Station'
觀音寺 (佐伯區).access_car: about 10 minutes from Sanyo Expressway 'Itsukaichi IC'

紫陽花苑 (光運動公園).tagline: Experience a breathtaking floral oasis with 30,000 vibrant hydrangeas in full bloom.
紫陽花苑 (光運動公園).desc:
Kou Undou Kouen is a vast hilly green space that beautifully combines sports facilities with rich nature. Located on the eastern side of the park, the "Ajisai-en" is one of the top flower viewing spots in Yamaguchi Prefecture, nestled against natural slopes and home to an impressive 30,000 hydrangeas that burst into bloom in June.

The main visual highlight is the "three-dimensional flower wall that spreads along the hillside." Stroll along the well-maintained pathways, where the lush, colorful flower walls rise almost to eye level. From the highest observation deck, you can take in the breathtaking expanse of this colorful floral carpet, creating a stunning visual impact.

The park is free to visit and features a large free parking area. Since the main hydrangea area is concentrated on sloped natural hillsides and stone steps, it is highly recommended to wear non-slip, comfortable sneakers for flower viewing. Arriving in the early morning allows you to capture the ethereal light that fills the air with a rich, natural ambiance.
紫陽花苑 (光運動公園).types: 30 varieties including Western, Edge, and Mountain hydrangeas
紫陽花苑 (光運動公園).access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR "Hikari Station"
紫陽花苑 (光運動公園).access_car: About 20 minutes from Sanyo Expressway "Kumage IC"

灌頂ヶ滝.tagline: Experience the majestic roar of nature at this hidden gem!
灌頂ヶ滝.desc:
Kanjōgataki, located in Katsurahama, is nestled next to the ancient temple Jigenji of the Koyasan Shingon sect, a historical site revered for its natural beauty. Every June during the rainy season, hundreds of wild hydrangeas bloom around the magnificent 80-meter waterfall and the main hall, creating a breathtaking scene.

The most awe-inspiring sight is the "majestic waterfall, mysterious rainbows, and a three-dimensional floral wall" all in one frame. As the waterfall cascades down with the force of galloping horses, sunlight refracts through the mist, often forming stunning rainbows. At your feet, blue-purple globular flowers bloom against the steep rock walls, exuding an ethereal aura.

The path to the main hall by the creek is mostly made up of natural gravel and steps, and the abundant moisture keeps the trails slippery year-round. Be sure to wear professional non-slip hiking shoes when visiting. This is a serene retreat away from the hustle and bustle, where early morning visits offer the purest air and perfect lighting for cultural photography.
灌頂ヶ滝.address: 徳島県勝浦郡上勝町正木
灌頂ヶ滝.access_train: Take a bus from JR Tokushima Station to the Kamikatsu area, then transfer to a taxi.

鴨部川紫陽花夢之路.tagline: Stroll through Sanuki's countryside, enchanted by thousands of blooms and pastoral landscapes.
鴨部川紫陽花夢之路.desc:
Kamoebegawa Ajisai Yume no Michi is a scenic pathway that stretches along both banks of the Kamoebegawa River in Sanuki City during early summer. This vibrant sea of flowers, lovingly maintained by local residents over the years, transforms the gentle countryside trail into a stunning spectacle every June.

The main feature is the "interplay of clear streams and blooming flowers." Water-blue and deep purple flower clusters bloom along the banks of the clear stream, beautifully complemented by quaint villages and lush green fields nearby. As a gentle breeze blows, the colorful blossoms sway, creating a picturesque Japanese early summer rural landscape.

The site is open to the public for free all day, and the flat riverside walking path is perfect for travelers looking for a leisurely morning stroll or bike ride. On sunny summer days, the midday sun can be glaring with little shade, so it's highly recommended to bring a sun umbrella. Visiting in the morning is the best way to capture the fresh air of the countryside.
鴨部川紫陽花夢之路.address: 香川県さぬき市造田是弘
鴨部川紫陽花夢之路.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from JR Zōta Station
鴨部川紫陽花夢之路.access_car: About 15 minutes from Shido IC on the Takamatsu Expressway

出釈迦寺.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of hydrangeas at the sacred foot of Ihaisanjin Temple.
出釈迦寺.desc:
Shushakaji is an ancient spiritual site, listed as the 73rd stop on the Shikoku Pilgrimage, nestled at the foot of the sacred Ihaisanjin Mountain. Every year during the rainy season from early June to mid-June, thousands of hydrangeas quietly bloom around the shrine and the ancient temple grounds, adding a soft touch of summer color to this solemn sacred space.

The most representative aspect of its Japanese aesthetic is the dialogue between the "wooden old shrine, stone lanterns, and the historical presence of blooming flowers." The elegant blue and purple flower clusters embrace the rustic worship hall and historical monuments, all under the shade of towering ancient trees, creating a perfect blend of tranquility and historical Zen unique to this ancient temple.

This is a pilgrimage site away from the hustle and bustle, offering a spacious and peaceful environment. The path to the 奥之院 consists of natural gravel and slightly steep steps, so wearing comfortable hiking shoes is recommended. Please maintain silence during your visit; weekday mornings are the best time for capturing the cultural landscape with fewer worshippers around.
出釈迦寺.address: 香川県善通寺市吉原町1091
出釈迦寺.access_train: About 10 minutes by taxi from JR Zenjiji Station
出釈迦寺.access_car: About 15 minutes from Takamatsu Expressway Zenjiji IC

道之驛 Karari 周邊.tagline: Capture the vibrant reflections of giant bridges and blooming flowers along the serene Oda River.
道之驛 Karari 周邊.desc:
道之驛「内子フレッシュパークからり」is a tranquil oasis that combines local agricultural products with the rich natural riverside scenery. Every June, thousands of hydrangeas bloom along the pathways beside the flowing Oda River, painting the entire park in a riot of colors.

The highlight not to be missed is the stunning interplay between the iconic Karari Bridge and the sea of flowers. Colorful blue and pink blooms sway gently in the breeze by the riverside, perfectly complementing the massive wooden suspension bridge that spans the clear stream. The calm water reflects the vibrant flower balls, exuding a refreshing early summer charm.

The park's pathways are well-maintained and gently undulating, making it very friendly for strollers and seniors. While enjoying the flowers, you can also savor the famous local 内子豚 dishes and fresh fruit gelato at the道之驛. On sunny summer days, it’s highly recommended to bring an umbrella and visit in the morning.
道之驛 Karari 周邊.address: 愛媛県喜多郡内子町内子2452
道之驛 Karari 周邊.access_train: About a 15-minute walk from JR「内子駅」
道之驛 Karari 周邊.access_car: About 5 minutes from Matsuyama Expressway「内子五十崎IC」

道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).tagline: Stroll along the ancient castle moat, savoring the classic summer hues of history and nature.
道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).desc:
Dogo Park is built on the historic site of the medieval castle, Yuzuki-jo, and is a natural park adjacent to Japan's oldest hot spring town, Dogo Onsen. Each year, during the rainy season from early June to mid-June, hundreds of hydrangeas quietly bloom along the old moat and the walking paths, adding vibrant colors to the ancient city as summer begins.

The park's core feature is its "retro moat waterfront, Edo-era earthen walls, and sea of flowers". The colorful blooms flourish beside the gentle waterway, and when the calm surface perfectly reflects the clusters of blue and purple flowers along with the restored samurai residences in the distance, the scene opens up, showcasing an elegant aesthetic of Japanese beauty.

The park's flat terrain is fully paved, making it ideal for independent travelers looking to enjoy a leisurely morning stroll after soaking in the Dogo Onsen. The park is open to the public all day, and reserving an hour for a walk is best done in the early morning on weekdays when crowds are minimal, perfect for capturing stunning reflections.
道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).count: Scattered throughout the park and surrounding streets
道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).address: 愛媛県松山市道後公園
道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).access_train: A short walk from the Iyotetsu tram stop, Dogo Onsen Station
道後溫泉周邊 (道後公園).access_car: Recommended to use nearby paid parking lots.

六條八幡宮.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of summer blooms at this historic shrine in Kochi City.
六條八幡宮.desc:
Rokujou Hachiman-gu is an ancient shrine nestled near the heart of Kochi City, rich in history and culture. During the rainy season in June, hundreds of hydrangeas quietly bloom around the old courtyard, shrine, and along the long gravel path, adding gentle colors unique to early summer to this sacred space.

The most representative aspect of Japanese aesthetics here is the dialogue between the wooden shrine, ancient torii gate, and the vibrant flowers. Colorful blooms cluster around rustic stone lanterns and towering ancient trees, and after a light rain, the water droplets on the petals make them even more vivid, perfectly embodying the tranquil Zen spirit of this historic site.

This hidden gem is away from the hustle and bustle, making it a perfect spot for travelers seeking a high-quality flower-viewing experience. The shrine features natural stone paths and a few steps, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. Please maintain silence while visiting, as weekday mornings are the best time for capturing the shrine's timeless beauty in photos.
六條八幡宮.address: 高知県高知市春野町西分3522
六條八幡宮.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kochi Station to 'Rokujou Hachiman-gu Mae'
六條八幡宮.access_car: Approximately 20 minutes from Kochi City center

加茂川親水公園.tagline: A tranquil riverside oasis, bursting with vibrant blooms and lush green lawns.
加茂川親水公園.desc:
Kamo River Shinsui Park is nestled along a resource-rich riverside, combining expansive green fields with clear streams in a comprehensive ecological park. Every early summer, vibrant hydrangeas bloom along the park's walking paths and waterside, painting the oasis with a splash of colorful summer hues.

The visual highlight is the interplay of "clear flowing springs, open lawns, and colorful flower walls." Strips of blue and purple flowers stretch alongside the gentle river, reflecting the clear blue sky and distant mountains in the pristine waters, beautifully complemented by the lush flower walls on the shore.

The park's well-maintained walking paths are flat and perfect for families looking for a relaxing early summer outing and picnic. Due to limited shade in the surrounding area, the midday summer sun can be intense, so it's highly recommended to bring sun protection and visit in the morning or before dusk for the most comfortable experience and softest light.
加茂川親水公園.address: 高知県土佐市高岡町乙
加茂川親水公園.access_train: About 40 minutes by bus from JR "Kochi Station" to the "Takaoka" area
加茂川親水公園.access_car: About 10 minutes from Tosa IC on the Kochi Expressway

紫陽花園 (山都町).tagline: Discover a hidden paradise where thousands of vibrant blooms meet the majestic Aso mountains.
紫陽花園 (山都町).desc:
Yamatocho Ajisai-en is nestled in the high-altitude valleys at the foot of the magnificent Aso outer rim mountains, offering a serene escape that perfectly blends with nature. Thanks to the cooler mountain climate, the blooming season here is later than in the plains, reaching its peak from late June to mid-July, with thousands of flowers quietly unfurling their beauty.

The visual highlight is the "dialogue between the deep, pristine mountain forest valley, the vintage suspension bridge, and the three-dimensional flower carpet." Stroll along the gently sloping paths, where colorful ribbon-like flower walls stretch alongside the clear streams, harmonizing with the lush Aso mountain range in the distance, showcasing an ethereal and pure aesthetic.

As the entire area retains its original rugged terrain, the walking paths are mostly dirt and gravel. It is highly recommended to wear sturdy hiking shoes and bring mosquito repellent when visiting. In the early morning, the valley often shrouded in mist creates a romantic and dreamlike atmosphere among the blooming flowers.
紫陽花園 (山都町).address: 熊本県上益城郡山都町米迫
紫陽花園 (山都町).access_train: Take a bus from Kumamoto Station to Yamatocho, approximately 1.5 hours.
紫陽花園 (山都町).access_car: About 40 minutes from the Kyushu Expressway, Mifune IC.

天念寺.tagline: Experience the ethereal beauty of flowers and ancient bridges in a sacred mountain retreat.
天念寺.desc:
Tennenji, located on the Kunisaki Peninsula, is a well-preserved spiritual site embodying the medieval practice culture of "Rokushō Mansan." In early summer, hydrangeas bloom quietly along the Nagaiwaya River gorge and around the main hall, adding a gentle blue-purple hue to this mysterious place of asceticism.

The most breathtaking visual highlight is the "dialogue between the cliffside Nameless Bridge and the sea of valley flowers." From the riverbank, gaze at the stunning stone arch, the "Nameless Bridge," perched atop steep, rugged cliffs, contrasting beautifully with the wildflower clusters cascading below. The expansive scene is both powerful and serene.

Accessing the main hall near the gorge involves natural dirt paths and gravel roads, so wearing good hiking shoes is recommended. This area is far from the urban hustle, with relatively few tourists. In the early morning, mist often envelops the mountains, creating a mystical atmosphere around the ancient temple and its flower sea, perfect for culture-loving travelers.
天念寺.count: Scattered along the river and within the temple grounds
天念寺.address: 大分県豊後高田市長岩屋1152
天念寺.access_train: About 20 minutes by taxi from JR "Usa Station"
天念寺.access_car: Approximately 30 minutes from "Usa IC" on the Higashi Kyushu Expressway

若宮八幡神社.tagline: Experience the enchanting blooms of 若宮八幡神社, where tradition meets vibrant summer colors.
若宮八幡神社.desc:
若宮八幡神社, located in the nostalgic Showa-era town of 豊後高田, is a historic center of faith. Each year during the rainy season from late June to mid-July, thousands of hydrangeas burst into bloom around the majestic torii gate, shrine buildings, and along the approach, painting the sacred grounds in a stunning summer palette.

The highlight here is the exquisite "Hanamizuharai"—a breathtaking display where colorful hydrangea blooms float in the handwashing basin and various water bowls, perfectly framed by traditional Japanese decorations. This serene scene beautifully captures the classical romance of Japanese rituals.

The shrine grounds are flat and easy to navigate, making it perfect for a day trip to explore the nearby Showa no Machi old streets. During the flower festival, special collectible hydrangea-themed omamori and limited edition goshuin are available. Visiting on weekday mornings offers a quieter experience, ideal for capturing pristine reflections in your photos.
若宮八幡神社.address: 大分県豊後高田市高田2863-14
若宮八幡神社.access_train: Take the JR train to "Usa Station," then transfer to a bus to "豊後高田" and walk for 10 minutes.
若宮八幡神社.access_car: Approximately 15 minutes from the Higashi-Kyushu Expressway "Usa IC."

開聞山麓交誼公園.tagline: Discover a floral paradise at the foot of Satsuma Fuji, where vibrant hydrangeas bloom in abundance.
開聞山麓交誼公園.desc:
