Kaimondanroku Kōen, located in Ibusuki City, is a recreational haven nestled at the base of the iconic Kaimondake mountain, blending cozy cabin camping with nature. From late June to mid-July, thousands of Western hydrangeas bloom along the grassy fields and pathways, painting the highland landscape with vibrant summer colors.

The park's core feature is the stunning juxtaposition of the majestic Satsuma Fuji scenery and the wildflower sea. The perfectly conical Kaimondake volcano, known as Satsuma Fuji, towers in the background, harmonizing beautifully with the colorful flower walls swaying in the breeze, showcasing the rugged romance unique to southern Kagoshima.

This hidden gem is beloved by outdoor camping and hiking enthusiasts. With limited shade, the midday summer sun can be intense, so it's highly recommended to bring an umbrella and stay hydrated. Visiting in the early morning allows you to capture the ethereal morning mist that enhances the grandeur of the volcanic flower sea.
開聞山麓交誼公園.address: 鹿児島県指宿市開聞十町2626
開聞山麓交誼公園.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR Ibusuki Makurazaki Line 'Kaimon Station'
開聞山麓交誼公園.access_car: Approximately 25 minutes from Ibusuki City center

東雲之里 (あじさい園).tagline: Step into Japan's premier hydrangea haven, where 100,000 blooms create a breathtaking mountain spectacle.
東雲之里 (あじさい園).desc:
東雲之里, nestled in the mountains of Izumi City, is a private ecological garden that showcases the stunning natural landscape. Spanning an impressive 40,000 square meters, this magnificent valley is home to around 100,000 hydrangeas, creating a visual impact that is truly astonishing when they bloom in July.

The most iconic experience here is the "maze of endless floral valleys." As you stroll along the natural trails carved into the hillside, you'll find yourself surrounded by a sea of wild hydrangeas in varying shades. At the summit, the vibrant floral carpet cascades like a colorful waterfall.

Since the entire area retains its original natural beauty, be prepared for a hike along steep paths to enjoy the flowers. It's essential to wear professional non-slip hiking shoes and carry mosquito repellent. At the foot of the mountain, there's a quaint pottery tea house, and we recommend setting aside at least two hours to fully appreciate this hidden gem.
東雲之里 (あじさい園).types: 160 varieties including Western and mountain hydrangeas
東雲之里 (あじさい園).count: approximately 100,000 plants (one of the largest in Japan)
東雲之里 (あじさい園).fee: 大人 500 円 (僅花期收取)
東雲之里 (あじさい園).address: 鹿児島県出水市上大川内2881
東雲之里 (あじさい園).access_train: About 20 minutes by taxi from JR Izumi Station
東雲之里 (あじさい園).access_car: Approximately 25 minutes from the Minami Kyushu Expressway "Izumi IC"

大隅湖 (高隈水壩).tagline: Discover the stunning beauty of 大隅湖, where vibrant flowers and a striking bridge create a picturesque escape.
大隅湖 (高隈水壩).desc:
大隅湖 is a vast, man-made reservoir embraced by mountains and breathtaking scenery. From mid June to early July, the park plants around a thousand hydrangeas along the gentle lakeside trails and surrounding waterfront park, creating a refreshing floral display by the water's edge.

The visual highlight is the "red bridge, lush green mountains, and colorful flower sea interwoven in a mirror-like reflection." Walls of blue and purple flowers line the clear lake, and when the tranquil waters perfectly reflect the iconic red bridge and vibrant flower clusters, the scene feels expansive and poetic.

The lakeside trails are well-maintained and easy to navigate, making it perfect for travelers seeking a leisurely morning stroll. During the blooming season, a lively local hydrangea festival takes place. For the best chance to capture a pristine, ripple-free reflection of the lakeside scenery, visiting on a weekday morning when the lake is calm is highly recommended.
大隅湖 (高隈水壩).address: 鹿児島県鹿屋市上高隈町
大隅湖 (高隈水壩).access_train: No direct train service; taking a taxi from central 鹿屋市 is recommended.
大隅湖 (高隈水壩).access_car: About 20 minutes from the 鹿屋串良 IC on the Higashi Kyushu Expressway.

本興寺.tagline: Visit Kanazawa's historic Nichiren temple and immerse yourself in the stunning sea of hydrangeas at the 'Hydrangea Temple.'
本興寺.desc:
Honkoji Temple, located in the heart of Kanazawa, is an ancient temple belonging to the Nichiren sect, renowned for its breathtaking floral displays, earning it the title of one of Hokuriku's premier 'Hydrangea Temples.' Every June, the path leading from the ancient mountain gate to the main hall becomes a vibrant tapestry filled with thousands of hydrangeas.

The quintessential Japanese aesthetic here is defined by the 'perfect harmony of the rustic stone path and the sea of flowers.' Vivid blue and purple blooms stretch from both sides of the stone pathway towards the center, creating a dreamy floral tunnel that beautifully contrasts with the ancient wooden structures and moss-covered Jizo statues, embodying a profound Zen spirit.

The grounds are flat and compact, making it easy for travelers of all ages to stroll leisurely. Since this spot is a highly popular destination during the blooming season in Hokuriku, it is strongly recommended to arrive early on weekdays right after opening to capture the serene beauty of the stone path adorned with flowers without any crowds.
本興寺.fee: 大人 500 円 (僅紫陽花祭期間)
本興寺.address: 石川県金沢市薬師町ロ75
本興寺.access_train: Take a bus from JR Kanazawa Station for about 20 minutes to 'Yakushi' stop.
本興寺.access_car: About 5 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway 'Kanazawa Morimoto IC'.

足羽神社.tagline: Discover a sacred realm steeped in 1500 years of history, where summer blooms softly embrace ancient wooden shrines.
足羽神社.desc:
足羽神社, perched atop 足羽山, is an ancient shrine revered by locals for over 1500 years. Each June, during the rainy season, hundreds of hydrangeas quietly bloom around the majestic torii gate and along the lengthy stone steps, painting the sacred grounds in soft summer hues.

The core visual experience is the harmonious blend of sacred traditional architecture and vibrant flowers. Colorful blooms cluster around the rustic wooden shrine, stone lanterns, and ema racks, all under the shade of towering ancient trees, showcasing a serene and spiritual Japanese aesthetic that refreshes the soul with every step.

The shrine is open to visitors free of charge all day, offering a spacious and tranquil environment. To reach the main hall, visitors must ascend an ancient stone staircase, so casual footwear is recommended. During the blooming season, the shrine offers exquisite hydrangea-themed omamori and limited edition goshuin. Mornings on weekdays see fewer visitors, making it the perfect time for photography.
足羽神社.address: 福井県福井市足羽1丁目8-25
足羽神社.access_train: 15-minute walk from 福井鉄道「足羽山公園口駅」
足羽神社.access_car: About 20 minutes from 北陸自動車道「福井IC」

あじさいロード (福井市).tagline: Wander along Ajisai Road and immerse yourself in a stunning floral corridor stretching for miles.
あじさいロード (福井市).desc:
Fukui City's "Ajisai Road" is a scenic corridor that stretches for several kilometers along the Asuwa Mountain Road, blooming beautifully in early summer. To honor the city flower, locals have planted thousands of hydrangeas along both sides of the road. Every June, this area transforms into a breathtaking, colorful floral tunnel.

The biggest visual charm lies in the "three-dimensional flower walls" that undulate with the mountain path. Whether you roll down your car window for a slow drive or take a leisurely stroll, vibrant blue and purple blooms are within arm's reach on both sides. The colorful floral display rises and falls with the terrain, creating a dynamic beauty that perfectly blends with nature.

As this spot is essentially an open roadway, please be mindful of passing vehicles while enjoying the flowers and finding the perfect angles for photos. It’s recommended to visit on weekdays during non-peak hours in the morning when the sunlight is gentle, making it ideal for photography and allowing for a safer, more leisurely experience.
あじさいロード (福井市).address: 福井県福井市狐橋1丁目〜東下野町
あじさいロード (福井市).access_train: About 15 minutes by bus from JR Fukui Station, get off at "Higashi Shimono"
あじさいロード (福井市).access_car: About 15 minutes from Hokuriku Expressway "Fukui IC"

樂山公園.tagline: Capture the breathtaking view of Mount Fuji amidst a vibrant sea of flowers.
樂山公園.desc:
Rakuzan Park is nestled on the hilly highlands of Tsuru City, offering a serene escape with stunning views of Mount Fuji. From late June to early July, thousands of hydrangeas bloom along the park's slopes and natural walking paths, painting this tranquil highland with vibrant colors.

The most iconic experience here is the "panoramic view of flowers and distant landscapes." As you leisurely ascend the gentle paths, colorful flower clusters embrace the lush greenery along the way. On clear days, you can capture the magnificent Mount Fuji framed beautifully alongside the colorful flower fields.

With relatively few visitors, this park is perfect for travelers seeking a peaceful and high-quality flower viewing experience, away from the crowds. The trails consist mainly of natural dirt paths with some steps, so wearing comfortable running shoes is recommended. Visiting at dusk not only offers a refreshing atmosphere but also allows you to capture the gentle beauty of the flowers bathed in the warm glow of the setting sun.
樂山公園.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Fujikyu Railway's Tsuru Bunkadaigakumae Station
樂山公園.access_car: About a 10-minute drive from Chuo Expressway's Tsuru IC

塩田平紫陽花小道 (あじさい小道).tagline: Stroll through the enchanting 'Shinshu Kamakura' and lose yourself in a vibrant floral paradise.
塩田平紫陽花小道 (あじさい小道).desc:
Shiodan Daira Ajisai Komichi is located in the historic Ueda City, known as 'Shinshu's Kamakura.' This scenic path stretches for several kilometers through lush countryside, cultivated by local residents who have planted thousands of hydrangeas, making it a classic hidden gem of early summer in Nagano.

The main feature is the 'colorful flower corridor along ancient irrigation ponds and canals.' Whether you walk or bike through, you'll be surrounded by vibrant blue and purple blooms within reach on both sides. The vivid floral walls, alongside the old salt ponds and the uniquely shaped Dokozan in the distance, create a stunning rural landscape.

As this path is an open public route, please be mindful of sun protection and watch for agricultural vehicles while enjoying the flowers and seeking the perfect angle for photos. It's ideal for planning a leisurely bike ride. Aim for a clear morning for the best visibility to capture the breathtaking scenery of the highland fields and hydrangeas perfectly.
塩田平紫陽花小道 (あじさい小道).access_train: About a 20-minute walk from Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line 'Shiodamachi Station'
塩田平紫陽花小道 (あじさい小道).access_car: About a 30-minute drive from the Joshinetsu Expressway 'Ueda Sugadaira IC'

飯山城址公園.tagline: Experience the perfect blend of nature and history at the ancient site of Iiyama Castle, surrounded by a sea of summer blooms.
飯山城址公園.desc:
Iiyama Jōshi Kōen is built on the historic site of Iiyama Castle, a strategic stronghold constructed by Uesugi Kenshin during the Sengoku period. This park beautifully merges nature and history. From late June to mid-July, the hydrangeas bloom vibrantly along the ancient castle walls and old moats, adding a splash of color to the majestic stone ramparts.

The visual highlight here is the harmony between the historical stone walls and the vibrant flowers. Strolling along the well-maintained paths, you'll see delicate blue and deep purple flower clusters reflecting against the enduring stone structures. After the rainy season, the petals glisten with droplets, enhancing the park's timeless beauty.

The park is built on the hilly terrain of the castle, so some paths have gentle slopes. It’s highly recommended to wear good non-slip sneakers while flower viewing. The spacious park offers plenty of shade, making it a comfortable retreat for summer strolls. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, perfect for leisurely walks and capturing stunning photos.
飯山城址公園.access_car: About 15 minutes from Joshinetsu Expressway Iiyama Toyoda IC

鶴舞公園.tagline: Discover Nagoya's century-old urban oasis, where French fountains meet the elegance of hydrangea pathways in early summer.
鶴舞公園.desc:
Tsuruma Park is Nagoya's first urban park, a historic garden that beautifully blends Japanese landscaping with Western aesthetics. Every June, the "Ajisai no Sanpomichi" on the southern side of the park reaches its peak, with over 2,300 hydrangeas quietly blooming along the serene riverbank.

The visual highlight is the elegant fusion of Western-style gardens and vibrant flowers. Colorful pink and blue blooms line the gentle stream and gravel paths, complementing the historic buildings and French fountain nearby. As the breeze dances across the water's surface, the scene becomes rich and refreshing, exuding the charm of early summer.

Conveniently located near the city center and completely flat, the park is perfect for travelers looking to enjoy a leisurely morning stroll. During early summer, the irises also bloom, so set aside an hour to wander. Early mornings on weekdays see the fewest crowds, making it the ideal time for photography and capturing the pristine reflections on the water's edge.
鶴舞公園.address: 愛知県名古屋市昭和区鶴舞1
鶴舞公園.access_train: Direct access from JR and Subway "Tsuruma Station"
鶴舞公園.access_car: About 5 minutes from Nagoya Expressway "Fukiage Nishi IC" or "Fukiage Higashi IC"

太江寺.tagline: Discover a thousand-year-old temple by the sea, where sacred halls meet ethereal hydrangeas.
太江寺.desc:
Taiouji, located beside the famous Futamiura, is an ancient mountain temple with over 1,200 years of history, belonging to the Shingon sect. It is renowned across Japan for its unique "pet blessings and offerings." In early summer, thousands of hydrangeas quietly bloom around the ancient courtyard and the approach to the rear mountain.

The visual highlight is the "sacred dialogue between the serene Japanese-style hall, Kannon, and wildflower clusters." Elegant blue and purple flower balls embrace the rustic wooden shrine, stone lanterns, and mossy pathways, all under the shade of towering ancient trees, showcasing a tranquil and spiritually rich Eastern aesthetic.

This hidden gem is incredibly pet-friendly, allowing travelers to bring their furry friends along for worship and flower viewing. The temple's terrain includes some stone steps, so comfortable walking shoes are recommended. Please maintain silence during your visit; weekday mornings are the best time for capturing the temple's timeless cultural scenery.
太江寺.address: 三重県伊勢市二見町江1659
太江寺.access_train: About a 20-minute walk from JR "Futamiura Station"
太江寺.access_car: Approximately 5 minutes from Ise Futami Toba Line "Futami IC"

大慈寺.tagline: Discover the serene beauty of Japan's first hydrangea temple, where ancient gates meet a sea of blooms.
大慈寺.desc:
Daijiji, nestled in the picturesque Shima Peninsula, is an ancient temple belonging to the Rinzai sect of Myoshinji. Known affectionately as the "Hydrangea Temple" of the Shima region, it boasts an impressive collection of hydrangeas, with up to 1,500 blooming in vibrant colors each June.  

The temple's most iconic scene features the perfect blend of the "vintage wooden gate and a tapestry of flowers." Colorful pink and blue blooms cascade along the ancient stone paths and steps, harmonizing beautifully with moss and stone monuments. This tranquil setting perfectly embodies the unique Zen atmosphere of the temple.  

The temple grounds are flat and compact, making it easy to explore without any strain, with a visit taking about thirty minutes. During the blooming season, the temple offers exquisite hydrangea-themed omamori and limited edition goshuin. Early morning visits on weekdays see fewer worshippers, allowing you to fully enjoy the ethereal environment and ideal photography lighting.
大慈寺.types: 30 varieties including Western,額, and mountain hydrangeas
大慈寺.address: 三重県志摩市大王町波切409
大慈寺.access_train: About a 10-minute walk from Kintetsu "Poche" bus stop
大慈寺.access_car: Approximately 50 minutes from Ise Expressway "Ise IC"

饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).tagline: Discover Japan's earliest blooming hydrangea paradise, crafted by a master gardener with 40,000 stunning blue blooms.
饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).desc:
Yohena Ajisai-en is nestled on a hillside in Motobu, Okinawa, a miraculous garden created over decades by the late "Uto Oba-san," renowned throughout Japan. Thanks to Okinawa's warm climate, this garden boasts the earliest peak bloom in Japan, starting in mid-May.

The most breathtaking sight here is the "three-dimensional blue ocean" that engulfs the entire hillside. As you stroll along the winding steps, you’ll find around 40,000 blue and deep purple hydrangea blooms sprawling across the natural slopes, transforming the mountain into a dreamlike deep blue wonderland.

Due to its steep terrain, the garden features noticeable elevation changes and narrow steps, so wearing non-slip, professional athletic shoes is highly recommended for flower viewing. May marks Okinawa's rainy season, and visiting on rainy days allows you to capture the most vibrant hues of the blue blooms, creating stunning, story-rich photographs.
饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).fee: 大人 500 円 / 中高生 200 円
饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).address: 沖縄県国頭郡本部町伊豆味1312
饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).access_train: It is recommended to take an express bus from Naha Airport to Nago Bus Terminal and then transfer to a taxi.
饒平名紫陽花園 (よへなあじさい園).access_car: About a 40-minute drive from Okinawa Expressway's Kyoda IC.

見歸之瀑.tagline: Experience the thunderous roar of Japan's top waterfall amidst a breathtaking floral tapestry.
見歸之瀑.desc:
Jingui no Taki, located in Karatsu City’s Ajimu Town, is one of Japan's top 100 waterfalls and a premier flower viewing destination in the Kansai region. Every June, the stunning deep valley and majestic cliffs come alive with a spectacular bloom of around 40,000 hydrangeas, making it the most impressive display in all of Kyushu.

The most breathtaking sight is the "majestic waterfall and the sprawling floral carpet in perfect harmony." The towering waterfall cascades down with the force of galloping horses, while at your feet, a deep blue wall of flowers spreads lavishly down the valley slopes, creating a striking visual contrast of power and grace.

Due to the steep, natural terrain of the area, the rocky walking paths tend to be damp and slippery throughout the year, so be sure to wear proper non-slip hiking shoes. During the flower season, enchanting night illuminations are held, and visiting early in the morning on weekdays is the best way to avoid large crowds and capture the ethereal scenery.
見歸之瀑.fee: 免費 (紫陽花祭期間需支付環境維護費：大人 500 円)
見歸之瀑.address: 佐賀県唐津市相知町伊岐佐
見歸之瀑.access_train: About 5 minutes by taxi from JR Chikuhi Line "Ajimu Station"
見歸之瀑.access_car: About 20 minutes from Nagasaki Expressway "Taku IC"
